We used a simple but recognisable shape (a flower) and focused on trying to stay within the lines and fill the gaps appropriately. Pasting in certain areas Choosing the right pieces and trying to keep in the lines - Take the time to talk about the shape. What is it? Where do you see this shape? etc. Talk about how you drew it or perhaps help the child draw it. - Extend this activity by using more shapes or colours. Have the child cut or rip the tissue paper themselves. - Simplify the activity by ripping or cutting the paper yourself and having your child do the pasting. - Some children need more encouragement than others. If all your little boy will do is play cars, then make your collage a car shape etc. Let them choose the colours they'll use etc. *We used tissue paper this time but obviously you can use any type of paper you have... some will be more difficult to rip/cut than others So we could write it and I could copy it down what you wrote, and then you could give him the copy of it. Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Jane. She was only 3 years old and liked to jog every day. One day, Jane decided to jog to the park. She was so excited to explore! When Jane arrived at the park, she saw something large and round. It was a pear tree! Jane saw lots of pears hanging, so she ran to them and started picking them. Suddenly, Jane heard a voice. It was an old man sitting on a bench. "Do not pick those pears, they are not yours," he said in a serious tone. Jane was scared, and she started to jog away. But then she noticed something on the ground and stopped. It was a pear that had fallen from the tree. "It's ok if I pick this one, right?" Jane asked the old man. The old man smiled and said, "Yes, you can pick this one, it's ok". So Jane picked the fallen pear and jogged home, happy to have something to eat. [2] The part communicated consisted of the first fourteen chapters. In revising these sheets for the press, it was necessary for the editor, in some places, to connect the more finished parts with the pages of an older copy, and a line or two in addition sometimes appeared requisite for that purpose. Wherever such a liberty has been taken, the additional phrases will be found inclosed in brackets; it being the editor's most earnest desire to intrude nothing of himself into the work, but to give to the public the words, as well as ideas, of the real author. An idol is someone who you look up to, admire, or even worship, and when they fall from grace, it can shake your beliefs and leave you feeling lost or disappointed. However, learning how to deal with this situation can help you move forward and grow as a person. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to handle a fallen idol: ### Step 1: Acknowledge Your Feelings When your idol falls, it's natural to feel a range of emotions such as shock, anger, disappointment, sadness, or betrayal. Allow yourself to feel these emotions without judgment. It's essential to acknowledge what you are experiencing rather than suppressing or denying your feelings. By acknowledging them, you give yourself permission to process them fully and start healing. Key tip: Don't judge yourself for having strong reactions. Everyone responds differently to situations like this, so allow yourself to grieve or express frustration if needed. ### Step 2: Get the Facts Straight Before making any hasty decisions or judgments about your idol, make sure that you have all the facts straight. Gather information from reliable sources and verify their credibility before accepting them as true. Avoid jumping to conclusions based solely on hearsay or social media rumors. This will prevent you from forming misguided opinions or taking unnecessary actions. Guideline: Be cautious of confirmation bias - our tendency to seek out information that confirms our existing beliefs while ignoring contradictory evidence. Try to approach the situation objectively by considering multiple perspectives. ### Step 3: Separate the Person From Their Accomplishments Your idol may have achieved great things in their career or personal life, but remember that people are complex beings capable of both good and bad deeds. When dealing with a fallen idol, try to separate their accomplishments from their character flaws or mistakes. Appreciating their work doesn't mean endorsing their wrongdoings. Maintaining this distinction allows you to continue enjoying their contributions without overlooking their shortcomings. Key tip: Focus on analyzing the art itself instead of focusing too much on the artist behind it. For example, enjoy listening to music without dwelling on the musician's personal issues. ### Step 4: Reevaluate Your Values and Beliefs Some people travel on their vacation, to visit other towns, cities, or countries. This is tourism. These people stay overnight in hotels, hostels, motels, apartments or bed and breakfast. Some prefer camping. Through the centuries, modes of travel have changed. [Chi is referring to a construction of three silver men stacked on top of each other] = = = children/CHIDES_NA/NewmanRatner/11/7061AS.cha = = = Title: How to Get Music onto Windows Movie Maker Windows Movie Maker is a discontinued video editing software developed by Microsoft. Although it's no longer being updated or supported, many users still find it useful for creating simple home movies or presentations. Adding music to your project can greatly enhance its overall impact, making it more engaging and enjoyable to watch. Here's our comprehensive guide on how to get music onto Windows Movie Maker. **Step 1: Choose Your Music Source** Firstly, you need to decide where you want to source your music from. This could be an audio file already saved on your computer, a CD, or even online streaming services like Spotify or YouTube. The choice depends entirely on what suits your needs best. If you have specific songs in mind that aren't available elsewhere, using existing files would be ideal. On the other hand, if you're looking for something new, consider checking out legal methods for downloading or streaming music. **Step 2: Locate the Audio File (if applicable)** If you've chosen to use an existing audio file, locate it within your computer's folders. Once found, remember the location as you will need to navigate back to this point later during the process. Common locations for media files include the 'Downloads', 'Music', or 'Documents' folder. **Step 3: Import the Audio File into Windows Movie Maker** Launch Windows Movie Maker and open your project. Click on the 'Add videos and photos' button which resembles a plus sign inside a square, usually located at the top right corner of the screen. Select 'Add music'. A new window will pop up prompting you to browse for your preferred track(s). Navigate to the previously identified location and select your chosen song(s), then click 'Open'. *Key Tip*: You can import multiple tracks simultaneously by holding down the Ctrl key while selecting them. However, keep copyright laws in mind when combining different pieces of work. **Step 4: Adjust the Audio Duration** After successfully adding the music, notice that the duration might exceed your actual video content. To adjust this, drag the edges of the audio clip along the timeline until they match the length of your visual material. Remember, trimming doesn't delete any part of the original song; instead, it only affects the portion visible within the movie maker. **Step 5: Fine-Tune Sound Levels** To ensure clear dialogue or sound effects don't get drowned out by background music, fine-tuning the sound levels becomes crucial. With the audio clip selected, look for the 'Volume' option under the 'Audio Tools' tab. From here, you can either lower or raise the volume depending on your requirements. *Guidelines*: As a rule of thumb, try keeping music around 70% volume so foreground sounds remain audible. Also, avoid drastic changes in volume throughout the piece to maintain consistency. **Step 6: Preview Changes** Finally, preview your creation to check whether everything aligns correctly - both visually and aurally. Use the playback controls provided to review your work before finalizing it. Don't forget to save often to prevent losing progress due to unexpected shutdowns or crashes. By following these steps, you should now have successfully added music to your Windows Movie Maker project! I was holding it [yellow wand] a little bit too... Imagine you have a funnel and a basketball. The funnel is like a cone, open at the top and pointed at the bottom. The basketball is like a sphere, round and even all around. Now let's say we want to find the volume of space where the funnel and the basketball overlap in the first quadrant. This means we're only looking at the area where x, y, and z are all positive. In math, we call this "cylindrical coordinates." To figure out the volume, we need to know the limits, or boundaries, of our shape in each direction. Since we're in the first quadrant, our angle (called theta) will go from 0 to 90 degrees (or 0 to pi/2 radians). But what about the other two dimensions, r (radius) and z (height)? For the radius, think about when the funnel (cone) and the basketball (sphere) meet. They touch when the distance from the origin (the center point) equals the height of the funnel. So, in cylindrical coordinates, the radius goes from 0 to the square root of 1 minus the square of the radius (we write this as $r \leq \sqrt{1-r^2}$). Now, for the height, imagine slicing the overlapping region into many thin circular disks (think of cutting a pie into smaller pieces). Each disk has a thickness along the z-axis, which ranges from the height of the funnel to the height of the basketball at any given radius. Writing this in math, we get $z$ going from the radius ($z = r$) to the square root of 1 minus the square of the radius ($z \leq \sqrt{1-r^2}$). So, there you have it! By understanding the shapes involved and breaking them down into manageable parts, we can tackle complex problems step by step. Even without knowing advanced mathematical concepts like integration, visualizing and reasoning through problems can lead us to answers! Kitty's not post bother us at the supper table. Then you touch the' other side an' you say two. Well then she hit her with the broom... It can be used in a mathematical proof about the thing. [Wal finds a toy car on the floor and starts to play with it] Determining assets in accounting is an essential task that provides valuable insight into a company's financial health. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the process step-by-step, ensuring clarity and practicality. By following these instructions, you'll be able to identify and classify assets accurately, enabling better decision-making and financial management. ### Step 1: Understand the Concept of Assets Assets are resources owned or controlled by a business that can provide future economic benefits. These resources can be tangible (physical) or intangible (non-physical). Examples include cash, buildings, equipment, patents, trademarks, and accounts receivable. Key tip: Remember that assets represent value and potential earning capacity for your business. Accurate asset classification ensures proper financial reporting and analysis. ### Step 2: Familiarize Yourself with Asset Classifications Assets are typically classified into three categories based on their liquidity (ease of conversion to cash): 1. Current Assets: These are expected to be converted to cash or used up within one fiscal year or operating cycle, whichever is longer. Examples include cash, marketable securities, accounts receivable, and inventory. 2. Non-current Assets (or Long-term Assets): These have a useful life exceeding one fiscal year or operating cycle. They are not intended for sale during regular operations but may be sold eventually. Examples include property, plant, and equipment (PP&E), investments, and intangible assets like patents and trademarks. 3. Other Assets: Some assets do not fit neatly into either current or non-current categories, such as prepaid expenses, deferred tax assets, and goodwill. Guideline: Always consider the nature of the asset and its expected usage when determining its classification. ### Step 3: Identify Potential Assets Review all documents related to your business transactions, including bank statements, invoices, purchase orders, contracts, and other relevant records. Look for items that meet the definition of an asset and could potentially provide future economic benefits. Key tip: Be thorough and meticulous during this step to ensure no assets are overlooked. ### Step 4: Verify Ownership or Control Confirm that your business has legal ownership or control over the identified items. For example, leased equipment might not qualify as an asset if the lease does not grant control to your business. Guideline: Consult with a qualified accountant or attorney if there's uncertainty about ownership or control of potential assets. ### Step 5: Measure Economic Benefits Estimate the future economic benefits associated with each asset. Consider factors such as the asset's remaining useful life, market conditions, and any applicable contractual obligations. Key tip: Use realistic and supportable estimates to avoid overstating or understating the value of your assets. ### Step 6: Record Assets in Financial Statements Once you've determined which items are assets and estimated their respective values, record them in your business's balance sheet according to their appropriate classifications. Make sure to comply with generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) or international financial reporting standards (IFRS), depending on your jurisdiction's requirements. Guideline: Regularly review and update your asset register to maintain accurate financial records. By diligently following these steps, you'll be well-equipped to determine assets in accounting and contribute to sound financial management practices for your business. The much higher amount of oxygen gas around Earth is because of the oxygen cycle. Photosynthesis takes hydrogen from water using energy from sunlight, which gives off oxygen gas. The hydrogen combines with carbon dioxide to make carbohydrates. Respiration then takes oxygen gas out of the atmosphere and turns it into carbon dioxide and water. The medlar is a tree and the name of the fruit of this tree. Its Latin name is "Mespilus germanicus", or "German medlar", but it is not from Germany. It grows in the Near East and South-Eastern Europe, and the Romans took it to Germany. Near the Caspian Sea, people grew the medlar about three thousand years ago. It came to Greece in about 700 BC and to Rome in about 200 BC. It was an important fruit plant in Roman and medieval times. But by 1600-1700, people started eating other fruits and not the medlar. Today people do not grow it very often. They start with Ben Nevis at about five o'clock. It had black and white fur and a long tail. "Hello, cat!" Ben said. "Can we share your shade?" The cat opened one eye and looked at them. It did not say anything. It closed its eye again and snored. Lila and Ben sat under the tree and watched the cat. They wondered what it was dreaming about. Maybe it was dreaming about mice or birds or milk. Suddenly, they heard a loud noise. It was a car. It had a flat tire and stopped near the tree. A man got out of the car. He looked angry and worried. "Oh no, how will I fix this?" he said. "I have to go to work soon." Lila and Ben felt sorry for the man. They wanted to help him. "Maybe we can help you fix your car," Lila said. "Yeah, we are good at fixing things," Ben said. The man smiled and thanked them. He gave them a jack and a wrench. They helped him lift the car and change the tire. It was hard work, but they did it. The man was very happy. He gave them a hug and a candy. He said they were very kind and smart. "Thank you so much, Lila and Ben," he said. "You saved my day." The cat woke up and stretched. It jumped down from the tree and rubbed against their legs. It purred and licked their faces. It said they were very brave and friendly. "Thank you too, cat," Lila and Ben said. "You shared your shade with us." They all smiled and waved goodbye. One day an invitation came from the palace to attend the grand ball the king was giving for his son the prince. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/NewmanRatner/Interviews/18/6337NK.cha = = = Title: Have Fun with an LPS (Little People Set) An Little People Set (LPS) is a popular toy brand produced by Fisher-Price that encourages imaginative play through small figures and accessories. This tutorial will guide you through various ways to have fun with your LPS while ensuring safety and promoting creativity. **Step 1: Familiarize Yourself with the Set** Take some time to explore all the pieces included in your set. Carefully unbox everything and lay out the figurines, buildings, vehicles, and other accessories. Familiarizing yourself with these components allows you to understand what's available for creative storytelling and roleplaying scenarios. Key Tip: Keep track of all parts after opening the box to avoid losing any items. **Step 2: Clean Hands Before Playing** Before handling the toys, make sure your hands are clean to prevent dirt or bacteria from transferring onto them. Encourage others who will be playing as well to do the same. Regular handwashing helps maintain good hygiene practices during playtime. Guideline: Follow CDC recommendations for proper handwashing techniques. **Step 3: Create a Safe Environment** Choose a flat surface free from sharp objects or edges where you can spread out your LPS setup. Clear away potential choking hazards if there are younger children involved. Always supervise young kids during playtime to ensure their safety. Safety Note: Discard any broken or damaged pieces immediately to prevent accidental ingestion or injury. **Step 4: Build Scenes and Settings** Construct settings using the provided buildings, landscapes, or furniture according to the manufacturer's instructions or based on your own imagination. Building scenes enhances spatial awareness and planning skills while fostering creativity. Creativity Boost: Use additional materials like blankets, pillows, or cardboard boxes to expand the play area and create new environments. **Step 5: Introduce Characters and Storylines** Incorporate the LPS characters into your scene and start developing stories around them. You could recreate real-life situations, invent fictional adventures, or even mix different themes together! Roleplaying helps develop empathy, social skills, and language abilities. Roleplay Guideline: Let each child choose their favorite character(s), encouraging cooperation and turn-taking when necessary. **Step 6: Add Sounds and Movement** Make sounds effects, voices, and movements for the characters and surroundings to bring the scene to life. Using descriptive words and phrases enriches vocabulary and communication skills. Voice Acting Tip: Practice imitating various accents, tones, and emotions to add diversity and depth to your characters. **Step 7: Document the Fun** Capture special moments by taking photographs or recording videos of the LPS action. Sharing these memories with family members or friends can spark conversations and inspire future playtimes. Photography Tip: Try using different angles and perspectives to capture unique views of the LPS world. **Step 8: Store Toys Properly** After playtime ends, pack up the LPS neatly in its original packaging or a designated storage container. Storing toys properly prevents damage and loss while teaching responsibility and organization skills. Organization Guide: Label containers clearly and encourage children to help put away their toys. By following these steps, you'll maximize the enjoyment and educational value of your LPS experience. Remember always to prioritize safety and cleanliness while nurturing creativity and imagination! He was playing in the park with his friends when he saw a twig that was very shiny. He rushed over to it and grabbed it quickly. "What is this?" Toby asked, looking at the twig in his hand. His friends gathered around to look too. "That looks like a special twig," said one of his friends. "It could be worth something." Toby was excited - he wanted to keep the twig for himself. But his friends insisted that he should share it. "Hurry up and share the twig," they said. Finally, Toby decided to share the twig with his friends and enjoy playing with them. He found out that the twig was quite common and it didn't hold any special value, but that didn't matter. All that mattered was that he did the right thing and shared it. The moral of this story is that sharing is more important than taking - it's better to enjoy the moment and play together with friends than to keep something special only for yourself. Mexico City was first built in the 1520s after Hern n Cort s destroyed the Aztec city of Tenochtitlan. The Spaniards then emptied the basin of Lake Texcoco to keep it from flooding. By the late 1700s, Mexico City had over 100,000 people. However, there were many slums, which caused epidemics. There were political problems in the mid 1800s after Mexico became independent. Between rounds, the players' hands "develop" in some way, often by being dealt additional cards or replacing cards previously dealt. During a round of betting, there will always be a "current bet amount", which is the total amount of money bet in this round by the player who bet last in this round. To keep better track of this, it is conventional for players to not place their bets directly into the pot (called "splashing" the pot), but rather place them in front of themselves toward the pot, until the betting round is over. When the round is over, the bets are then gathered into the pot. Instead you put the r in between! [pushes his car into something on the table] I'm goin' put some water in this XXX. Title: How to Do a Hair Mask for Frizzy Hair Frizzy hair can be difficult to manage, but with the right hair care routine, you can significantly reduce frizz and enjoy smooth, shiny, and healthy-looking locks. A hair mask is an excellent addition to your hair care regimen as it provides deep conditioning and nourishment that regular shampooing and conditioning may not offer. This step-by-step guide will teach you how to do a hair mask specifically designed for frizzy hair. **Time required:** 20 minutes - 1 hour (depending on hair length and thickness) **What you'll need:** * A high-quality hair mask formulated for frizzy or dry hair * A wide-tooth comb * An applicator brush or shower cap (optional) * A mild sulfate-free shampoo * A microfiber towel or old t-shirt for drying ## Step 1: Choose Your Hair Mask Well can I make up a name? You have to be patient and listen to what I tell you, or else you will suffer the consequences. Lucy was very sorry for her mistake and learned her lesson. The difference between integers is an integer. The product of integers is an integer (for example, (12 + 2345 x (67 - 8) ) x 9 is an integer). All turtles have common parts which classify all of them to be turtles. First of all, every turtle has a carapace, which is the top shell, and they have a plastron, which is the bottom shell. Scutes are scales that cover both shells, and they are made of keratin like fingernails. Like humans, turtles also have necks. They can fold their neck into their shells in an s-shape. The turtle's nostrils are near the top of the head so the turtle won't have to stick its head very far out of the water to breathe. A turtle has no teeth, but its beak makes up for it because it catches, holds, and slices food. On a car or boat, a servo is usually used for steering. Model sailboats use a servo to adjust the sail as well as the rudder, or steering device. On airplanes and helicopters, servos are used not only for steering but for up and down pitch. If the model uses an engine to make it run, a servo is used to control the speed of the engine. A model which operates with an electric motor usually has something called an electronic speed control or "ESC" to control its speed. Who goes scratch scratch against the fence? CHAPTER 2 Earnestly as Maria endeavoured to soothe, by reading, the anguish of her wounded mind, her thoughts would often wander from the subject she was led to discuss, and tears of maternal tenderness obscured the reasoning page. She descanted on "the ills which flesh is heir to," with bitterness, when the recollection of her babe was revived by a tale of fictitious woe, that bore any resemblance to her own; and her imagination was continually employed, to conjure up and embody the various phantoms of misery, which folly and vice had let loose on the world. The loss of her babe was the tender string; against other cruel remembrances she laboured to steel her bosom; and even a ray of hope, in the midst of her gloomy reveries, would sometimes gleam on the dark horizon of futurity, while persuading herself that she ought to cease to hope, since happiness was no where to be found.-But of her child, debilitated by the grief with which its mother had been assailed before it saw the light, she could not think without an impatient struggle. "I, alone, by my active tenderness, could have saved," she would exclaim, "from an early blight, this sweet blossom; and, cherishing it, I should have had something still to love." In proportion as other expectations were torn from her, this tender one had been fondly clung to, and knit into her heart. I like water and milk and iced tea. It wasn't that I didn't take you seriously. Problem: A company wants to make cylindrical containers (like cans) to hold a specific amount of stuff. The top and bottom of each can are made from a special, more expensive material, while the curved sides are made from a cheaper material. They want to know the best shape for these cans - specifically, the ratio of height to width that will make the can the cheapest to produce. Let's think through this together! 1. Imagine you're designing a cylindrical container. You already know its volume, but adjusting both the height and the base radius will change the costs. So, our main goal here is finding the perfect height (let's call it "h") and base radius ("r") combo that keeps expenses minimal. 2. To keep things easy for everyone, let's say the total volume (V) of your container is calculated using the classic cylinder formula: V = r h. Our job now is to find out how changing "h" and "r" affects the overall cost without altering the container's volume. 3. Cost is determined by two factors - one part comes from making the circular tops and bottoms (which we agreed is N times pricier than the sides), and another portion depends on crafting the curvy walls. Let C represent the price per unit area of the less expensive wall material. Then, the combined cost of manufacturing the top and bottom is NC multiplied by the area of just ONE circle (as there are two). That means the cost of materials for ONLY the top and bottom equals NC r . 4. Now, calculate the cost of creating the side walls. Walls of a cylinder consist of rectangles wrapped around, so their area is simply length width. In our case, the 'length' refers to the circumference (2 r), while the 'width' relates to the height (h). Hence, the cost of the sidewall material adds up to Ch (2 r) h. 5. Finally, remember that the TOTAL cost combines BOTH parts. Add the prices of constructing the top-bottom duo AND the sidewalls: Cost = NC r + Ch (2 r) h 6. As grade-schoolers, we want to minimize this cost equation while keeping the volume constant (remember, V = r h). We could try different values for 'h' and 'r', but it'd take forever! Instead, let's use some smart tricks from math whizzes called "calculus." These techniques help us quickly discover the ideal height-radius pair! 7. Using calculus, we learn that when trying to minimize a value, we need to set its derivative equal to zero and solve for our variables. After doing all those calculations (don't worry-you'll study them later!), we finally arrive at the magical height-to-base-radius proportion that saves the most moolah: *drumroll*... h/r = N! And there you have it! By following these steps, you've learned how big companies save money on production while ensuring quality and meeting customer needs! Remember, math is everywhere, helping us make sense of real-world problems like building better canisters! That must be the new word for tree is trunk. [Nina walks around on the floor strewn with furniture from the doll house.] Title: How to Be a Good Neko Atsume Trainer Neko Atsume is a popular mobile game where you collect and interact with stray cats by leaving food and toys in your virtual yard. The goal is to attract as many unique cats as possible while maintaining their happiness and health. Here are some steps and tips to help you become an expert Neko Atsume trainer: **Step 1: Understand Your Cats' Needs** Each cat has its own preferences when it comes to food and playtime. Some may prefer sashimi over deluxe tuna, or fancy playing with a ball instead of a scratching post. Pay attention to which items each cat interacts with and what they leave behind in your yard (fish, silver fish, gold fish) to determine their favorite foods and toys. This will increase their chances of returning to your yard more frequently. **Key Tip:** Keep track of each cat's preferred items using notes or a spreadsheet for easy reference. **Step 2: Plan Your Yard Layout Strategically** The placement of food, toys, and furniture can greatly affect the number and variety of cats that visit your yard. Place high-value items such as the Luxury Bath and Mansion Shelter near the entrance to lure in new visitors quickly. Arrange lower-value items further into the yard to encourage exploration and interaction between cats. Additionally, consider placing multiple instances of the same item together to maximize attraction potential. **Key Guideline:** Always test different layout configurations to find the most effective arrangement for attracting various cats. **Step 3: Rotate Items Regularly** To keep your feline friends interested and engaged, regularly rotate the items available in your yard. Remove less popular items and replace them with new ones every few days. This will prevent boredom and ensure constant engagement from your cats. **Key Tip:** Make sure to monitor the impact of removing certain items on specific cats; if they stop visiting after removing one of their favorites, reintroduce it promptly. **Step 4: Manage Food Supply Efficiently** Carefully manage the amount and type of food offered in your yard. Higher quality food tends to be more expensive but also results in higher rewards. Balance cost efficiency with the desire to attract rare and valuable cats. Also, remember to refill food bowls before they run empty to maintain visitor satisfaction. **Key Guideline:** Monitor daily expenses versus income earned through fish collection to optimize feeding strategies. **Step 5: Observe Cat Interactions** Pay close attention to how cats behave towards each other within your yard. If two cats appear hostile towards one another, separate them by adjusting your yard layout or providing additional hiding spots. Conversely, if friendly interactions occur, try arranging items to promote these relationships and potentially unlock special scenes. **Key Tip:** Learn each cat's personality traits and quirks to better understand their social dynamics. **Step 6: Collect Rewards Frequently** Collect rewarded fish daily to avoid losing any due to overflow. Save up larger amounts of silver and gold fish to purchase exclusive items that cannot be obtained otherwise. Utilize the bank feature wisely - depositing excess fish during periods of low spending allows for later withdrawals when desirable purchases arise. **Key Guideline:** Establish a regular routine for collecting rewards and monitoring expenditures. By following these steps and applying the provided tips and guidelines, you'll become a proficient Neko Atsume trainer capable of attracting and nurturing a wide variety of felines in your digital sanctuary. Happy training! [he reaches into the container and brings out the pitcher] And you're not going to take it from me. A circle moon going to be like that like. You can write it to me if you want. Come here you want me to help you? THE MEANING OF DECIMALS A decimal -- 0.3865 -- is a proper fraction, which is a number less than 1. It is a part of number 1. To understand what that means, we must introduce the ordinal numbers: first, second, third, fourth, and so on. Because the ordinal numbers express division into equal parts. In this Lesson, we will answer the following: The counting-numbers have two forms: cardinal and ordinal. A cardinal number answer the question How many? An ordinal number answer the question Which one? We will now see that the ordinal numbers express division into equal parts. An ordinal number names which part -- the third part, the fourth, the fifth, and so on. Division into equal parts If we divide 15, for example, into three equal parts -- into three 5's -- then we say that 5 is the third part of 15. We say that because 15 is the third multiple of 5: 5, 10, 15. We use that same ordinal number to name the part. If we divide a quantity into four equal parts, then we call each part a fourth; if into five equal parts, a fifth; if into one hundred equal parts, a hundredth; but if into two equal parts, we say half. And so with the exception of half, an ordinal number indicates into how many parts a quantity has been divided. A third, a fourth, a fifth, and so on, are the names of parts. Those ordinal numbers belong to language itself. They are not yet the names of numbers we call fractions, which, we are about to see, are numbers we need for measuring, and are the parts of number 1. We will go into this more in Lesson 15. The decimal system Since our numbering system is based on the powers of 10, it is called a decimal system. Decem in Latin means ten. In the previous Lessons we learned about whole numbers, which are the repeated additions of 1: First , we will divide One into ten equal parts, and so each part is called a Tenth. If we divide each Tenth into ten equal parts, then One will be in one hundred equal parts -- count them And so we have divided One into Hundredths. If we divide each Hundredth into ten equal parts, then each tiny piece will be a Thousandth part of One. And so on. Those Tenths, Hundredths, Thousandths are called the decimal units. The sequence of names of the decimal units follows the same sequence as the powers of 10. They are those powers in their ordinal form. The decimal point signifies "and," in the sense of "plus." That is the whole number 412 plus 3 tenths of 1, plus 8 hundredths of 1, plus 7 thousandths; and so on. Any number written with a decimal point is loosely called a decimal. A number less than 1 -- a proper fraction -- written as a decimal, we call a decimal fraction. A number written with a numerator and denominator we call a common fraction (Lesson 20). The positions to the right of the decimal point are called the decimal places; the 1st, the 2nd, the 3rd, and so on. Each decimal digit occupies a decimal place. The digit at the 1st decimal place shows the number of Tenths. In the number above, there are 3 tenths. The 2nd decimal place shows the number of Hundredths. In that number, there are 8 hundredths. And so on. The first digit to the left of the decimal point -- the 2 -- shows the number of Ones. As with whole numbers, each digit has its place value: 2.3875. . . Ones, tenths, hundredths, thousandths, ten-thousandths, . . . As with the whole numbers, each place is ten times the place to its right. One is ten tenths. One-tenth is ten hundredths. One-hundredth is ten thousandths. One-thousandth is ten ten-thousandths. And so on. Example 1. In this number a) there are how many ones? Answer. 4. The ones place is the first digit to the left of the decimal point. b) How many hundredths? Answer. 6. Hundredths (ordinal) is a decimal unit. It falls to the right of the decimal point. c) How many hundreds? Answer. 5. Hundred (cardinal) is a whole unit. It falls to the left of the decimal point. 534.267 = 5 Hundreds + 3 Tens + 4 Ones + 2 Tenths + 6 Hundredths + At this point, please "turn" the page and do some Problems. Continue on to Section 2. No you are going to stay here? No put it up in a ponytail so you look like a little girl XXX. Title: Understanding Simple Sequences and Their Sums Have you ever heard about sequences or their sums? No worries if you haven't! We are going to explore these mathematical ideas using a fun and familiar example. Imagine you have a pile of identical blocks. Now, let's say you start taking some blocks away, always two at a time from the top. The first time, you take away one block by itself; then a group of two blocks; followed by another set of two blocks, and so on. This process creates a sequence of remaining blocks: Block tower heights: 1, 1, 2, 2, 3, 3, ... You may notice that the number of blocks decreases by one every other level, forming a pattern: 1, then 2 blocks, then 1 block again, then 2 blocks, and so forth. Can you see how many blocks will be left on level 10? Or level 100? It gets difficult to keep track after a while, doesn't it? That's where understanding limits comes in handy! Now, instead of looking at just one block tower, imagine having infinitely many towers stacked up next to each other with the same pattern of leftover blocks. In mathematics, we call this arrangement of towers an "infinite series." How could we possibly calculate the total number of blocks in all those infinite towers combined?! Let's create a new but similar problem that has a known answer. Consider removing just one block from the top of your tower each time, resulting in this sequence: New block tower heights: 1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 0, ... It seems pretty clear now that no matter how far you go along the sequence, there will never be more than one block at any level since you remove them individually rather than in pairs. So, even with infinitely many towers lined up together, you still only have at most ONE BLOCK per tower level when considering the entire infinite array. Therefore, despite having infinitely many levels, the grand total of blocks remains FINITE-specifically, ONE BLOCK PER TOWER LEVEL across all the towers. Returning to our initial problem involving pairs of blocks taken off the tops of the towers, although individual tower levels contain varying numbers of blocks, the difference between consecutive levels eventually becomes very small due to the pairing system. As you keep adding more and more towers, calculating the exact sum becomes challenging, but intuitively, thinking back to our single block removal scenario, you might guess that the final sum won't grow beyond bounds either. Indeed, mathematicians discovered that this type of series actually approaches a finite value determined by the nature of its underlying pattern. So, even without getting into complex math techniques, you can understand the concept behind evaluating intriguing patterns like these. Just remember that sometimes even infinite processes can result in manageable outcomes! Are you going to sit on Santa's lap? Look at that he's takin' off in a box! Step 1: Meet the Prerequisites Before you can become a forklift driver in Victoria, Australia, you need to meet certain prerequisites. You must be at least 18 years old, have a current drivers license, and possess good eyesight and hearing. Meeting these requirements will ensure that you are legally eligible to operate a forklift and can hear and see well enough to do so safely. Step 2: Choose the Right Forklift Course There are different types of forklifts, and each type requires specific training. High-risk work licenses in Victoria offer courses for order pickers, reach trucks, pallet jacks, high reach trucks, and more. Research the different options and choose the course that best suits your needs and goals. This will help ensure that you receive the right training for the job you want. Key Tip: Some employers may require you to have a specific type of forklift license, so make sure to check with potential employers before choosing a course. Step 3: Enroll in a Training Program Once you've chosen the right forklift course, enroll in a training program. Look for programs offered by registered training organizations (RTOs) approved by WorkSafe Victoria. These programs should include both theory and practical components, as well as assessment tasks to demonstrate your competency. Completing an accredited program will give you the skills and knowledge needed to operate a forklift safely and efficiently. Guideline: Make sure the RTO you choose is reputable and has positive reviews from previous students. Step 4: Study and Prepare for Assessments Study the materials provided by the RTO and prepare for assessments. The assessments will test your knowledge and skills related to forklift operation, safety procedures, and maintenance. Passing these assessments is crucial for obtaining your high-risk work license. Key Tip: Take notes during classes and review them regularly. Practice using a forklift under supervision if possible. Step 5: Undergo Medical Examination If required, undergo a medical examination to ensure you are physically fit to operate a forklift. A medical examination may involve checking your blood pressure, heart rate, lung function, and vision. Passing the medical examination will confirm that you are capable of performing the physical demands of operating a forklift. Step 6: Apply for a High-Risk Work License After completing the training program and passing the assessments, apply for a high-risk work license through WorkSafe Victoria. You will need to provide proof of identity, evidence of completion of the training program, and payment of the relevant fee. Once your application is processed and approved, you will receive your high-risk work license. Guideline: Keep a copy of your high-risk work license with you at all times while operating a forklift. Step 7: Maintain Your Skills and Knowledge To maintain your high-risk work license, you must complete a refresher course every five years. Additionally, stay up-to-date with any changes in regulations or industry standards related to forklift operation. Regularly practicing your skills and seeking opportunities for professional development will help ensure that you remain a safe and effective forklift operator. Key Tip: Consider joining a professional organization related to forklift operation to access resources and networking opportunities. By following these steps and guidelines, you can become a qualified forklift driver in Victoria, Australia. Remember to prioritize safety and continue learning throughout your career to maximize your success and job satisfaction. She drinks more than you drink and you drink an awful lot. Title: How to Concatenate Text in Microsoft Excel Concatenation is the process of combining two or more text strings into one single string in Microsoft Excel. This can be particularly useful when you want to merge data from different cells into one cell. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to concatenate text in Excel. **Step 1: Open Your Excel Spreadsheet** Start by opening your existing Excel spreadsheet or creating a new one if necessary. Make sure the data you wish to concatenate is organized appropriately within separate cells. **Step 2: Understand The Concatenate Function** Excel uses the CONCATENATE function (or & operator) to join texts together. The syntax for this function is `=CONCATENATE(text1,[text2],...)`. Each piece of text that you want to combine should be enclosed in double quotes (" ") and separated by commas. If any space is required between combined texts, include a comma followed by a space in the formula like so: ", ". Alternatively, instead of using the CONCATENATE function, you could also simply use an ampersand (&). The format would look something like this: `=A1&" "&B1`, where A1 and B1 are the cells containing the individual pieces of text. **Key Tip**: Both methods yield identical results; however, some users find the ampersand method quicker due to its shorter length. **Step 3: Input The Concatenate Formula** Click on the cell where you want the concatenated result to appear, then type '=' to begin the formula. Now click on the first cell containing the initial part of the text, followed by typing a comma. Click on the next cell containing the second portion of the text, followed by another comma. Continue this pattern until all desired parts are included. Remember to add spaces as needed with additional commas. For example, if we have names distributed across columns A, B, and C (first name in A, middle initial in B, last name in C), and we wanted to combine these into full names in column D, our formula might look like this: `=A1&", "&B1&" "&C1` 'n' we can have even more XXX. Given that the snippet involves multiplying a number by a sum of a real number and an imaginary number, we can approach this concept using familiar concepts like grouping and distributive property. Here's an example: --- Imagine you have 5 apples and 7 oranges. Your friend comes along and gives you 3 more fruits - but instead of giving you one type of fruit, she gives you 3 apricots. Now, how would you figure out how many total fruits you have now? One way to think about it is to first find out how many fruits you had before your friend gave you the apricots (which is 5 apples + 7 oranges). Then, add the 3 apricots to that amount. In math, we write this as: Total Fruits = (Apples + Oranges) + Apricots = (5 + 7) + 3 = 12 + 3 = 15 In this expression, parentheses are used to show which numbers should be added together first, just like how you would eat the apples and oranges separately before eating the apricots. This idea is similar to what happens when we simplify expressions involving addition and multiplication in algebra. Now let's try another problem: Simplify $3(4+4i)$. The expression inside the parentheses represents two parts - a real part ($4$) and an imaginary part ($4i$). To simplify the expression, we need to distribute the $3$ to both parts, just like how we added the apricots to our total fruits earlier. We get: Simplified Expression = $3 \times 4 + 3 \times 4i$ = $12 + 12i$ So, $3(4+4i)$ simplifies to $12 + 12i$. Just like how we kept track of different types of fruits, we also keep track of real and imaginary numbers separately until we reach the final answer. My bangs are stickin' way way way... Title: How to Draw the Olympic Symbols Introduction: The Olympics is an international event that brings together athletes from all around the world. The symbol of this prestigious competition is the Olympic rings, which represent unity among five continents. This tutorial will guide you through the process of drawing these iconic symbols in a simple yet accurate manner. By following these steps, you'll be able to create your own version of the Olympic rings. Materials Needed: 1. Paper 2. Pencil 3. Eraser 4. Ruler (optional) 5. Colored pens or pencils (for adding color) Step 1: Understand the Design Before starting to draw, understand what the Olympic rings look like. They consist of five interlocking circles - blue, yellow, black, green, and red - arranged horizontally with the blue ring at the top. Each circle touches two others, one at its top and another at its bottom. These colors were chosen because every nation has at least one of them in their flag. Step 2: Sketch Out Guidelines Using your pencil, lightly sketch horizontal lines across your paper as guides for where the tops and bottoms of the circles should go. Then divide the space between them into fifths using either your eye or a ruler if you prefer precision. This gives us roughly equal spaces for our rings. Key Tip: Keep your guideline light so they can easily be erased later without damaging your final product. Step 3: Draw the Blue Circle On the first division counting from the top, draw a perfect circle using your pencil. You can trace around a round object or use a compass if available. Make sure it fits within the guideline divisions. Step 4: Draw the Yellow Circle Directly below the blue circle, draw another circle slightly smaller than the first one but still touching it. Fill it with yellow color. Step 5: Draw the Black Circle To the left of the middle line, position the next smallest circle such that it touches both the blue and yellow ones. Color it black. Step 6: Draw the Green Circle Now place the second largest circle to the right side of the centerline, ensuring it also touches both the blue and uppermost yellow circle. Fill it with green color. Step 7: Draw the Red Circle Finally, locate the remaining gap and fit the last and smallest circle there, connecting it to the lower yellow and black circles. Add red color to complete the set of rings. Key Guideline: Remember, no gaps exist between any pair of adjacent circles; they only touch along their edges. Step 8: Refine Your Drawing Go over your drawing carefully, checking proportions and connections. Correct any errors found during inspection. Once satisfied, gently rub out the guidelines underneath the colored areas. Step 9: Add Finishing Touches For added realism, consider shading parts of the overlapping regions to indicate depth perception. Also, ensure none of the background shows through by neatening up the outer edges of the rings. Congratulations! With careful practice, patience, and adherence to these instructions, you have successfully drawn the Olympic symbols. Share your creation proudly knowing you've captured a piece of global sports history. Title: Addressing Issues with Your Mother-in-Law: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Dealing with issues in any relationship can be challenging, especially when it comes to your mother-in-law. This guide will help you identify common problems and provide actionable steps towards addressing them constructively. Remember that open communication, empathy, and respect are essential throughout this process. Step 1: Identify the Issue(s) Key Tip: Be specific about what's bothering you. Vague complaints won't lead to productive conversations or solutions. Before addressing the issue, clearly define what the problem is from your perspective. Is she overstepping boundaries? Does she criticize you often? Once you have identified the core issue, gather examples to support your claim without exaggerating or being petty. Step 2: Reflect on Your Role Key Guideline: Consider how your actions might contribute to the conflict. Take some time for introspection. Are there instances where you may have unintentionally hurt her feelings or disrespected her opinions? Understanding your part in the situation can help facilitate a more understanding conversation. Step 3: Choose an Appropriate Time and Place Key Tip: Make sure both parties are calm and undistracted. Find a quiet, comfortable location free from distractions. Schedule a time when neither of you feels rushed or stressed. Starting the conversation during a family gathering could escalate tension and make things worse. Step 4: Use "I" Statements Key Guideline: Speak from your point of view instead of blaming or accusing her directly. Instead of saying, "You always interfere in our lives," say something like, "I feel overwhelmed when I see changes made to my plans." Using "I" statements reduces defensiveness and encourages active listening. Step 5: Explain How Her Actions Affect You Key Tip: Focus on your emotions rather than attacking her character. Articulate how her behavior impacts you emotionally. For instance, if she frequently criticizes your parenting style, express that it makes you feel inadequate as a parent. Avoid generalizing or making sweeping assumptions about her intentions. Step 6: Listen Actively Key Guideline: Show genuine interest in her thoughts and feelings. After sharing your concerns, invite her to share hers. Maintain eye contact, nod occasionally, and ask clarifying questions to show you're engaged. Resist the urge to interrupt until she finishes speaking. Step 7: Validate Her Feelings Key Tip: Recognize that her perception matters too. Even if you don't agree with her views, validate her right to feel the way she does. Phrases such as "I understand why you would think that" or "That sounds difficult" can go a long way in fostering mutual respect and understanding. Step 8: Work Together Towards a Solution Key Guideline: Seek compromise instead of insisting on getting your own way. Brainstorm potential resolutions together. Each party should give and take. If one solution doesn't work out, revisit the discussion and try another approach. Conclusion: Remember, building strong relationships takes effort. By following these steps, you demonstrate maturity, respect, and love - qualities that strengthen familial bonds despite challenges. However, keep in mind that not all conflicts get resolved immediately; patience and persistence are vital. Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy who was very confused. He didn't understand why he couldn't play with his toys all day, every day. His mom noticed that he was feeling confused and asked him what was wrong. Timmy responded, "I don't know why I can't play all the time. I'm just a youth." His mom smiled and said, "Let's discuss this. You can't play all the time because you need to learn new things too. Playing is fun, but learning is important too." Timmy thought about it for a moment and said, "Okay, I understand now. I'll play and learn too!" From that day on, Timmy was happy and no longer confused. He played with his toys and also learned new things every day. In fact just the other day my grammar gave her a bite of ice cream, and she spat it out. Chant is written in neumes, which are notes sung on a single syllable. Gregorian Chant has no meter at all, though it does have a rhythm of groups of 2 or 3 notes. Vertical lines separate musical phrases and may sometimes allow a pause for taking a breath, like Chant is not in a major key or a minor key, but in modes (though there are some modes which can sound like a modern scale). Chant is written on a 4-line staff, instead of 5 lines as music is written on now. marks where Do is on the staff. Here it is on the third line from the bottom, so if Do is on C then the lines would go F-A-C-E. would mean that Do is on the top line, so if Do is on C the notes on the lines would be D-F-A-C. is a Fah Clef, and indicates where Fah is on the staff. Here, Do would be on the bottom space. This is just a single note This is the same as a punctum. When one note is written above another note like this, the bottom note is sung first, and then the note above it. When the higher note comes first, it is written like this. Three or more notes going upward. Three or more notes going upward, but the middle one has a vertical episema: that note is slightly lengthened. Three or more notes going downwards. |Torculus (pes flexus ) Three notes that go up and then back down. |Porrectus (flexus resupinus) A high note, a low note, and a high note. The line starts at the first note and goes down to the middle note. Four notes, going up and then dropping down. A porrectus with a low note on the end. The opposite of a scandicus flexus. One note up and two notes down. Four notes in a row, going downwards. Four notes in a row, going up. |Epiphonus (liquescent podatus) Little notes lose some of their fullness because they are sung on a complicated syllable. |Cephalicus (liquescent flexa) In liquescent neumes that look like this, The top note comes before the bottom note! |Pinnosa (liquescent torculus) Again, the top note comes before the note underneath. The little note always comes last. The little note is highest. This is marked by a jagged line in the middle. The first note is held a little longer than the middle one. These are other ways of showing that a note is held: One is by putting a dot (punctum-mora) after the note. It is a little bit like a dotted note in modern music. The second way of showing that a note is held is by having more than one of the same note in a row on the same syllable. This is called a repurcussive neume. A horizontal line (episema) above a neume means to hold the note, or slow down, a little like rit. in modern music. A vertical line (episema) written under a note means it has a mild emphasis, like an accent mark. There is one accidental that may be used in Chant notation, it is the B-flat which does look a lot like the modern Sometimes the flat sign can be written at the beginning instead of in front of the note, and then it's like a key signature. Otherwise, it only lasts for one word. At the end of a line of chant, a little, skinny note (custos) is written to show what note is coming up next in the following line. And that is how to read Gregorian Chant notation! Then you ask them to apply it in a different way and oh my...it's time to reteach. This year I have been trying to get in a lot of variations for all kinds of learners. I love play dough and the kids love play dough so away we go with play dough for tens and ones. I simply had the children to roll ten tiny balls of play dough and then mash the ten tiny balls together to make one. They did this in math centers. The children enjoyed the activity and it gave them a concrete representation of the singles transitioning into one. The next time we do this I am going to have them decompose it after they put roll it into one. They will be happy to see play dough in the math centers. Who knew learning could be this fun? Title: How to Add an Exercise on Fitbit Fitbit is a popular fitness tracking device that allows you to monitor your daily physical activities, sleep patterns, heart rate, and much more. One of its features includes manually logging exercises performed throughout the day. This can be useful if you forget to wear your Fitbit during a workout or want to track an activity that isn't automatically detected by the device. Here is a comprehensive guide on how to add exercise on a Fitbit: ### Part 1: Logging Exercises via Fitbit App (Mobile) #### Step 1: Download and Open Fitbit App Download the Fitbit app from Google Play Store or Apple App Store depending upon your smartphone's operating system. Sign in using your credentials or create a new account if necessary. #### Step 2: Navigate to Today Tab Once logged in, look at the bottom menu bar where several options are listed such as Dashboard, Challenges, Notifications etc., Tap on 'Today'. Graphing a Linear Equation Using slope intercept form is one of the quickest and easiest ways to graph a linear equation. Before we begin, I need to introduce a little vocabulary. We are going to talk about x and y intercepts. An x intercept is the point where your line crosses the x-axis. The y intercept is the point where your line crosses the y-axis. We are only going to focus on the y intercept in this lesson, but you'll need to know x intercept for later. As you work through these examples, if you find that you are really having difficulty, take a look at the Algebra Class E-course. You will have instant access to more examples on video, a lot of practice problems and step-by step answer keys. It may truly be what you need to be successful with graphing equations. Let's look at an example: Slope intercept form is used when your linear equation is written in the form: y = mx+b x and y are your variables. m will be a numeral, which is your slope. b will also be a numeral and this is the y-intercept. In this form only (when your equation is written as y = ....) the coefficient of x is the slope and the constant is the y intercept. Let's look at a few examples and I promise that you'll LOVE this new way of graphing! Need a little more clarification? No problem, just check out the following video. Example 1 will be explained again step by step. Here's a quick summary of this lesson: Tip: You have to be very accurate in plotting your points and drawing your lines in order to be able to read your graph to find other solutions! Type in your equation and click "Draw". Sign Up for Algebra Class E-courses "I'd like to start off by relaying my sincerest gratitude for your dedication in teaching algebra. Ah hah oh what d' ya thinks underneath the pants? Loops are things that keep on repeating and repeating until you tell it to stop. There are two types of loops that we'll be learning: for-loops and regular loops. For-loops increase the value of an integer i (or anything else) by a given amount (default: 1), until it has reached its upper limit. for i : 1 .. 10 %Start with 1, end with 10, increase i by 1 (i.e. 1,2,3...10) put i end for That's all there is to it! Whatever's in the middle will be executed each time the loop is run. This program will print all numbers from 1 to 10. Now there are a few things you may have noticed. Firstly, we haven't declared i. Actually, you're not supposed to declare for-loop variables. In fact, if you do, then it won't work! What if we want to print all multiples of 5 between 1 and 103? for i : 0 .. 103 by 5 put i end for Excellent! You're now a for-loop guru! Loops are pretty simple, and the syntax is almost the same. So why would you want to use normal loops instead of for-loops? For-loops are used when you know how many times to repeat it. However, sometimes the amount of times you repeat depends on how many lines there are in a text file. More often, you'll want a program to loop until the user tells you to exit. This is what we'll be doing. var word : string loop put "Enter any word you want, and I'll tell you what it is! (type 'exit' to exit)" get word exit when word = "exit" put "You entered ", word end loop That's pretty self explanatory. We're getting a string from the user, and if the string is exit, then we exit. Turing is a very easy program to use, and it avoids complicated syntax, which becomes handy in cases like this; 'exit when' means exactly that, you exit when the following case is true. As the group travels east over the Misty Mountains, they are attacked by Goblins (Orcs). The Goblins take the dwarves and Bilbo into their caves. Gandalf saves them and the group flees. During their flight Bilbo gets lost, and tries to find a way out by himself. In the tunnels Bilbo finds a ring, which he puts in his pocket. By an underground lake, Bilbo meets the creature Gollum. Gollum and Bilbo play a game of riddles. If Bilbo wins the game, Gollum will show him the way out; if Gollum wins, he will eat Bilbo. When Bilbo does not know another riddle to ask Gollum, he finds the ring in his pocket and asks himself: "What have I got in my pocket?" Gollum thinks this is a riddle, but cannot answer it, and so Bilbo wins the game. Gollum is angry and does not want to help Bilbo. Gollum then goes away to get something he owns: a magical ring, which turns the wearer invisible. Gollum cannot find his ring, and realizes that Bilbo has it. Gollum goes back, but Bilbo is afraid and runs away. While running, the ring slips on Bilbo's finger and he becomes invisible. Gollum runs past Bilbo, towards the way out of the caves. Bilbo realizes that the ring makes him invisible. With the help of the ring he can follow Gollum and get out of the caves. Outside, Bilbo again meets Gandalf and the dwarves. Swimming has been known amongst humans since prehistoric times; the earliest record of swimming dates back to Stone Age, from around 7,000 years ago. Competitive swimming started in Europe around 1800 and was part of the first modern 1896 Summer Olympics in Athens, though not in a form comparable to the contemporary events. It was not until 1908 that regulations were implemented by the International Swimming Federation to produce competitive swimming. Title: How to Get to World 4 in New Super Mario Bros New Super Mario Bros is an exciting platform game that challenges players to navigate through various worlds filled with obstacles, enemies, and power-ups. To reach World 4, you must first complete Worlds 1, 2, and 3. This guide will provide a comprehensive walkthrough for reaching World 4 while offering valuable insights, strategies, and tips along the way. **Step 1:** Complete World 1 *Explanation*: Before accessing World 4, you need to finish World 1 by defeating Bowser Jr., rescuing Princess Peach, and watching the cutscene where she gets kidnapped again (unfortunately). Completing this world unlocks further levels and worlds within the game. *Key Tips*: * Collect as many coins as possible during your playthrough; they can be helpful later on. * Remember to take advantage of power-ups like Mushrooms, Fire Flowers, and Stars when available. They grant abilities that help overcome specific challenges or defeat tougher foes more efficiently. -------------------------------------------------------- **Step 2:** Finish World 2 *Explanation*: After finishing World 1, move forward to conquer World 2's eight stages and boss fight against Pom Pom. Successfully completing these tasks allows progression towards World 4. *Guidelines*: * Be prepared for underwater stages which require precise movement due to slippery controls. Use Propeller Suits wisely to gain altitude quickly and avoid hazards. * Make sure to explore hidden paths leading to secret exits; some may lead directly into Warp Cannons transporting you closer to World 4. -------------------------------------------------------- **Step 3:** Conquer World 3 *Explanation*: Now it's time for World 3! Overcome its eight unique stages and win the battle against Koopa Kid at the end. Defeating him clears the pathway to World 4. *Strategies*: * Keep an eye out for Mini-Mushrooms, Ice Flower, and Penguin Suit opportunities - all offer distinct advantages depending upon level design and enemy types encountered. * Watch out for Thwomps, Dry Bones, and Piranha Plants - common adversaries in this world requiring strategic approaches for safe passage. -------------------------------------------------------- **Step 4:** Access World 4 *Explanation*: With three castles down, only one remains - Castle 4. Clear its stage and face off against Bowser once more. Upon his demise, congratulations! You have reached World 4. *Additional Information*: * Unlike previous castles, there are no mid-stage checkpoints here. Proceed cautiously but swiftly to preserve lives. * Utilize Power-Ups strategically before entering battles with bosses; increased strength might make short work of them. In conclusion, getting to World 4 in New Super Mario Bros requires perseverance, skill, and knowledge about different elements found throughout the game. By following our steps above, exploring alternate routes, utilizing power-ups effectively, and understanding individual enemy behaviors, you too can advance deeper into the Mushroom Kingdom and enjoy everything else this fantastic title has to offer. Happy gaming! Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to walk with his cane. He would take it with him everywhere he went. One day, Tim went for a walk in the park. Eating new things can be an exciting and enriching experience! It allows you to discover new flavors, textures, and cultures while also providing nutritional benefits from a diverse range of foods. Here's a comprehensive guide on how to eat new things: **Step 1: Research Different Cuisines and Foods** Before trying something new, research various cuisines and dishes from around the world. This will help you understand what types of ingredients are used and give you ideas about which ones interest you most. Look up recipes online or visit local ethnic restaurants to sample different options. Reading books about food culture is another great way to learn more about global culinary traditions. *Key tip*: Start by exploring regional variations of familiar foods (e.g., Mexican vs. Italian pizza). **Step 2: Identify Potential Allergens or Dietary Restrictions** If you have any allergies or dietary restrictions, make sure to check whether the new food contains potential triggers. Many countries have their own labeling laws regarding common allergens like nuts, dairy, gluten, etc. However, if you're unsure, don't hesitate to ask questions when dining out or purchasing packaged goods. *Guideline*: Always carry an epinephrine auto-injector if you know you have a severe allergy. **Step 3: Try Small Portions** When introducing a new food into your diet, start with small portions to allow your palate time to adjust. You might find that you enjoy the taste after a few bites even if it was initially off-putting. Additionally, this approach minimizes waste if you end up disliking the dish. *Key tip*: Pair the unfamiliar ingredient with familiar favorites to ease transition. **Step 4: Savor Each Bite** Take your time eating and focus on enjoying every aspect of the meal - appearance, aroma, texture, and flavor. Chew slowly and thoroughly to fully appreciate the sensory experience. Not only does this enhance satisfaction, but it also promotes healthy digestion. *Guideline*: Avoid distractions such as TV, phone, or computer screens during meals. **Step 5: Be Open-Minded** Be prepared to encounter tastes and textures that may differ significantly from what you're accustomed to. Maintain an open mindset; remember that cultural preferences shape our perceptions of food. What seems strange at first could become a beloved staple over time. *Key tip*: Recall previous experiences where your initial reaction changed upon repeated exposure (e.g., coffee, beer, sushi). **Step 6: Learn Cooking Techniques** Learning how to prepare new dishes expands your repertoire and increases confidence in experimenting with novel ingredients. Attend cooking classes, watch instructional videos, or read cookbooks to develop skills relevant to specific cuisines. *Guideline*: Practice makes perfect - keep trying until you master the technique. **Step 7: Share Your Experience** Discuss your adventure with friends, family, or coworkers. Sharing stories about unique dishes and flavors fosters connection and encourages others to try new things too. Plus, hearing positive feedback from loved ones reinforces your willingness to continue broadening your horizons. *Key tip*: Document your journey through photos, journal entries, or social media posts. Remember, expanding your palate doesn't happen overnight. Patience, curiosity, and persistence are essential components of successfully incorporating new foods into your diet. Happy tasting! Latin is an Italic language that was used in Ancient Rome. Short Latin texts have been found from about the 5th century BC and longer texts from about the 3rd century BC. Inside a woman's breast are 15 to 20 sections called lobes. Each lobe is made of many smaller sections called lobules. Lobules have groups of tiny glands that can make milk. After a baby is born, a woman's breast milk flows from the lobules through thin tubes called ducts to the nipple. Fat and fibrous tissue fill the spaces between the lobules and ducts. The breasts also contain lymph vessels. These vessels are connected to small, round masses of tissue called lymph nodes. Groups of lymph nodes are near the breast in the underarm (axilla), above the collarbone, and in the chest behind the breastbone. Cancer begins in cells, the building blocks that make up tissues. Tissues make up the breasts and other parts of the body. Normal cells grow and divide to form new cells as the body needs them. When normal cells grow old or get damaged, they die, and new cells take their place. Sometimes, this process goes wrong. New cells form when the body doesn't need them, and old or damaged cells don't die as they should. The buildup of extra cells often forms a mass of tissue called a lump, growth, or tumor. Tumors in the breast can be benign (not cancer) or malignant (cancer). Benign tumors are not as harmful as malignant tumors: - are rarely a threat to life - can be removed and usually don't grow back - don't invade the tissues around them - don't spread to other parts of the body - may be a threat to life - often can be removed but sometimes grow back - can invade and damage nearby organs and tissues (such as the chest wall) - can spread to other parts of the body Breast cancer cells can spread by breaking away from the original tumor. They enter blood vessels or lymph vessels, which branch into all the tissues of the body. The cancer cells may be found in lymph nodes near the breast. The cancer cells may attach to other tissues and grow to form new tumors that may damage those tissues. Today we are going to learn to play "Happy Birthday". It is a familiar song to very many people. We are going to learn to play it as an exercise on developing your ear. There are many cooler songs out there that I'm sure you'd like to play, but this is about developing your ear. Let's start on D and sing the song in our head. "Happy birthday to you". Here is where we use intervals. It just goes up a bit. D D E D G F# are the notes of the fist verse. Now we want to try and figure out our key. The last note of that verse doesn't really give us a resolved feeling. If we sing the song in our head, we'll notice that the second time the line is sang, it ends differently. The second time the phrase ends with A G instead of G F#. The G at the end of the second line gives us that resolved feeling. That is a clue that we are singing the song in the G major scale. Another clue is that the song has an F# and so does the G major scale. Since we started singing on D, we know that the first note of the song is the fifth note of the scale of the key that the song is in. Let's finish picking out the melody using our ears. We end up with D D, then D an octave up, B, G, F#, E, then for the next line C, C, A, G, A, G. So, with the melody figured out let's write some chords. Normally a song starts on the root chord, so let's start with a G chord. When the melody gets to the F#, the chord wants to change. Let's see which chords have an F# in them. D and B minor both do. If we try both of them, we find that the D sounds a little better, so let's use that one. We can return back to the root chord when the melody returns back to a G. Next the melody is going to come to an F#. We are going to change our chord there, but the F# is sort of a passing note, so let's target our chord change on the following note, the E. Let's try a C chord with that part of the melody, the C chord has an E in it. Then we can go back to the G chord. Let's finish off with a D chord, which will drive our ear back to the root chord when we repeat. Visit the Piano Covers section for a complete list of song tutorials. Vegetarianism is the practice of not eating meat or fish. People who follow vegetarianism are called vegetarians. Vegetarians eat foods like vegetables, fruit, nuts, beans and grains. Some vegetarians eat or drink animal products, like milk and eggs. They are called lacto-ovo vegetarians. Vegetarian people may like tofu, falafel and other meat substitutes. He described Ladislas as a fugitive, unprotected by any Government; he bore about his person certain papers which had been found in the palace of Warsaw, and were the confidential communications of the Russian Autocrat to his brother the Viceroy of Poland, and were of such a nature as to rouse all Europe against their writer. These papers had been entrusted to Ladislas, whose intention was to proceed to Paris and publish them there. Private business, however, of the greatest importance, had forced him to visit Naples first. The Russian Government had traced him to Naples, and had empowered a certain Russian lady to take any step, or go any lengths, in order to obtain these papers from Ladislas. This lady had made Giorgio her emissary; her name he carefully concealed, but Marietta averred, from his description, that it could be no other than the Princess Dashkhoff. It's essential to understand that this article does not promote an unhealthy lifestyle but rather encourages self-acceptance, confidence building, and social skills development regardless of one's size. Step 1: Embrace Your Body Explanation: The first step towards becoming popular as a larger person is accepting and loving yourself just the way you are. This acceptance acts as the foundation for building your confidence and projecting a positive image. Key Tips: * Practice positive affirmations about your body every day. * Surround yourself with supportive friends who appreciate you for who you are. * Avoid comparing yourself to others; everyone has their unique beauty and worth. Step 2: Dress Comfortably yet Stylishly Explanation: Clothing can significantly impact how we feel about ourselves and how others perceive us. By finding fashionable clothes that fit well and make you feel good, you'll exude confidence and draw people towards you. Key Guidelines: * Invest in quality clothing that flatters your figure. * Explore different styles until you find what suits you best. * Don't shy away from bold colors or patterns - they can help you stand out in a crowd! Step 3: Develop Social Skills Explanation: Being able to engage in meaningful conversations and build rapport with others is crucial when trying to become more popular. Improving your social skills will allow you to connect with various individuals and create lasting impressions. Key Tips: * Practice active listening during conversations. * Show genuine interest in what others have to say. * Ask open-ended questions to keep discussions flowing smoothly. Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a pretty crystal necklace that she loved to wear. One day, Lily accidentally dropped her necklace and it broke into tiny pieces. She felt sad and her heart felt empty. But then, her mommy came and gave her a big hug to comfort her. She wiped away Lily's tears and said, "Don't worry, we can fix your necklace."
