Title: Catch Fish with Your Hands Introduction: Catching fish with your hands is an ancient technique used by our ancestors for survival. It requires patience, stealth, and understanding of aquatic life. This guide will teach you the necessary steps to successfully catch fish using only your hands as fishing gear. Please note that this method may not be allowed or legal in certain bodies of water, so always check local regulations before attempting to catch fish without proper equipment. Step 1: Observe the Water Body Before getting into the water, spend some time observing the body of water where you plan to catch fish. Look for signs of fish activity such as ripples, swirls, or splashes. Pay attention to underwater structures like rocks, weeds, or drop-offs which can provide cover and attract fish. Also, take notice of the general water conditions - clear, murky, fast moving, slow moving - all these factors influence fish behavior. Key Tip: The best times to try hand fishing are usually at dawn and dusk when light levels are low and fish are more active. Step 2: Choose Appropriate Clothing Wear clothing that provides comfort, flexibility, and protection from potential hazards (like sharp objects). Quick-drying materials are ideal since they reduce weight once wet. Avoid bright colors; instead opt for natural tones that blend well with the environment. Neoprene waders offer excellent insulation and buoyancy but remember safety first - never enter deep water alone or without proper flotation devices. Guideline: Always prioritize safety over convenience. Even if you're just waist-deep in water, unexpected currents or slips could lead to dangerous situations. Step 3: Approach Quietly Approaching quietly reduces the chances of scaring away fish. Move slowly and avoid making loud noises. Enter the water gently to minimize waves. Once submerged, move deliberately towards likely hiding spots, keeping your movements smooth and controlled. Key Tip: Practice mindfulness techniques while approaching fish. Focus on your breath and maintain awareness of every movement you make. Step 4: Locate the Fish Once near suspected hiding places, look carefully for any sign of fish. They might be partially hidden behind rocks, logs, or plants. Patience is key here. Wait patiently until you spot movement before proceeding. Step 5: Position Yourself Correctly When you see a fish, position yourself so that you approach from downstream if possible. This allows the current to carry your scent away from the fish. Keep a low profile to remain less visible. Step 6: Make Your Move When close enough, reach out quickly but calmly. Try to touch the fish lightly on its tail rather than grabbing it directly. A gentle tap often startles them momentarily, giving you enough time to seize them properly. Key Tip: Use both hands whenever feasible. One hand secures the fish around its body while the other supports its belly. Step 7: Handle the Fish Carefully After catching the fish, handle it gently to prevent injury. Hold it horizontally, avoiding contact with gills and scales. If releasing the fish back into the wild, do so promptly after ensuring it's healthy and capable of swimming strongly away. Conclusion: Hand fishing demands persistence, caution, and respect for nature. While successful catches bring excitement, remember that failure is part of the learning process too. As you gain experience, you'll become better equipped to read fish behaviors and react accordingly. Happy hand fishing! [repositions the two silver men, which are now standing one on top of the other, in another space on the base] Title: How to Change the Background on a Google Presentation Google Slides is a powerful tool that allows users to create professional-looking presentations. One way to make your presentation stand out is by customizing the background. This tutorial will guide you through changing the background on a Google Slide with clear, detailed steps. **Step 1: Open Your Google Slides Document** Start by launching Google Slides and opening the document whose background you want to change. If you haven't created one yet, start a new project. **Step 2: Navigate to the Slide Master View** The Slide Master view gives you control over slide layouts and masters. To access it, click on "View" in the top menu bar, then select "Master." A panel will appear on the left side of the screen showing all slides and their associated masters. **Key Tip:** Changes made in the Slide Master affect every slide based on that master. Therefore, changes here save time if you have multiple similar slides. **Step 3: Select the Appropriate Slide or Master** Determine which slide(s) or master(s) need a new background. Click once on the thumbnail image representing them. Remember, any adjustments now will be applied to all selected slides or those linked to the chosen master. **Step 4: Access the Background Options** With the correct slide or master highlighted, look at the 'Background' section within the right-hand pane. Here, you can choose from predefined themes, colors, images, or even videos as backgrounds. **Step 5: Choose a Solid Color Background** To pick a solid color, simply click the colored box next to 'Color.' A dropdown palette appears; select your preferred hue. Alternatively, enter a hexadecimal value (e.g., #FFFFFF for white) into the text field above the color options. **Why Use Solid Colors?** They provide simplicity, making text more readable against contrasting colors. Plus, they match brand identity when aligned with company guidelines. **Step 6: Opt for an Image Background** For something more dynamic, upload an image using the 'Image' option under 'Background.' You can either Upload from computer, Drive, Photos, or By URL. After selecting an image, resize or reposition it as needed via the crop icon and boundary boxes. **Guideline:** When choosing an image, consider its resolution. High-resolution images prevent pixelation upon scaling up. Also, ensure any text remains legible against complex patterns or dark tones. **Step 7: Customize Transparency & Tiling** Adjust transparency levels using the slider beneath the color/image selector. For tileable images, tick the checkbox below titled 'Tile this background.' **Step 8: Save Changes** Once satisfied with your selections, return to Normal View by clicking 'Slide' > 'Close Master View.' All modifications should now be visible across the applicable slides. In conclusion, personalizing your Google Slides background adds visual appeal while enhancing communication effectiveness. Follow these eight easy steps to transform ordinary presentations into engaging visual stories! At school today, they're going to go swimming. I want you to eat your chicken. You can eat fast but not too fast. Title: How to Draw a Smiley Face with a Nose **Introduction:** Welcome to this comprehensive drawing guide! Today, we'll be walking you through the process of creating your own smiley face with a nose. This simple yet delightful artwork can brighten up anyone's day and is an excellent starting point for beginners looking to improve their sketching skills. **Step 1: Gather Your Materials** You will need: - A pencil (HB or B grade) - An eraser - Paper or a sketchbook - A black pen or marker for outlining (optional) **Step 2: Lightly Sketch Guidelines** Using your pencil, draw two intersecting lines - one horizontal and one vertical - forming four quadrants on your paper. These lines serve as guides to help position facial features evenly and proportionally. They are easy to erase later so don't worry about making them dark. **Step 3: Draw the Basic Shape of the Head** On top of the vertical line, sketch an oval shape which extends just above and below the intersection of the two guideline lines. This forms the basic structure of the head. **Step 4: Position the Eyes** Divide the upper half of the face into roughly equal parts by drawing another horizontal line across the guideline. Then, draw two circles slightly overlapping this new line. Each circle should be slightly smaller than the width of the head. These create the eyes. **Step 5: Add Pupils and Highlights** Within each eye, place a smaller circle to represent the pupil. To indicate light reflection, leave some white space near the top part of each eye - these become highlights that give your smiley face a more lively appearance. **Step 6: Create the Nose** Just below the eyes, mark a spot where the tip of the nose would be. Now draw a curved line extending from each side of the head meeting at this midpoint. The curve should start wide at the sides then narrow towards the center, resembling a triangle without its base. **Step 7: Form the Mouth** Underneath the nose, draw a broad, flattened letter 'U'. Make sure there's enough room between the bottom lip and chin. Curve the ends of the mouth upwards to ensure our smiley face looks happy. **Step 8: Refine Facial Features** Go back over your initial sketches, refining shapes and sizes if necessary. Ensure all elements align with your original guidelines. Erase any visible guidelines at this stage. **Step 9: Outline & Shade** If desired, trace over your finalized pencil marks using a fine liner or marker. For added realism, lightly shade around the irises of the eyes, under the eyebrows, along the bridge of the nose, beneath the lower lip, and anywhere else shadows might naturally occur. **Key Tips and Guidelines:** - Practice regularly to enhance your drawing abilities. - Don't rush; take your time to observe correct proportions. - Use soft pressure when sketching initially, allowing easier corrections. - Comparing drawn objects against real ones can significantly boost accuracy. Congratulations! You now know how to draw a smiley face with a nose. Keep practicing and exploring different styles until you develop your unique artistic voice. Happy drawing! Creating an equation in a Google Doc can be done using the built-in drawing tools or by inserting equations from external sources such as MathType or EqnEditor. This tutorial will focus on creating equations using the built-in drawing tools, which is a simple yet effective method that allows you to create professional-looking equations with ease. Step 1: Open your Google Document To begin, open your Google Drive account and create a new document or open an existing one where you would like to add the equation. Step 2: Access the Drawing Tools Once your document is open, click on "Insert" located at the top menu bar. From there, select "Drawing" and then choose "New." A blank canvas will appear, giving you access to various drawing tools including shapes, lines, text boxes, and more. Key tip: If you have already created drawings before, they'll show up under "From Drive," so make sure to select "New" for this process. Step 3: Choose Shapes for Mathematical Symbols Google Drawings doesn't come with predefined mathematical symbols, but fear not! You can create them using basic shapes available in the toolbar. Here are some common ones: * Fraction line: Use two horizontal lines (Line > Line type > Horizontal) stacked vertically (Rotate). * Square root symbol: Find a rectangle shape, stretch it wide horizontally, and reduce its height significantly. Flip it vertically if needed (Format > Rotate > Flip vertical). * Radical sign: Similar to square roots, just extend the width even further. Remember, practice makes perfect. The more you work with these tools, the easier it becomes to formulate complex mathematical expressions. Step 4: Add Text Boxes for Variables and Numbers After setting up the necessary symbols, we need to input our variables and numbers. To do this, simply click on the "Text box" icon in the toolbar and draw a box over any part of your diagram. Type inside the box to enter your variable or number. Guideline: Make sure your font size is consistent throughout your equation for better readability. Step 5: Align Elements Properly For a cleaner look, align all elements properly within your equation. Select an element, then hover near another until arrows appear directing towards the edges. Click and drag accordingly to snap elements together neatly. Step 6: Group Components Together When satisfied with individual components, group them together to maintain alignment when moving parts around. Select multiple objects by holding down shift while clicking each piece, then right-click and hit "Group". Step 7: Save & Insert into Document Finally, once everything looks good, click "Save and Close" at the upper right corner of the drawing window. Your newly created equation should now appear embedded within your Google Doc. Additional Tips: * Keep it Simple: Avoid cluttering your workspace unnecessarily. Stick to what's required to convey your point clearly. * Consistency: Maintain consistency in sizing, spacing, and styling across different equations. * Reusable Templates: Once you get comfortable, consider saving frequently used templates directly to Google Drive ("File">"Save to Drive") for quick future reference. By following these steps and keeping these additional tips in mind, you're well on your way to mastering equation creation in Google Docs. Happy writing! Find an AirTag in Your Car: A Comprehensive Step-by-Step Guide AirTags are small, coin-shaped devices that can be attached to items like keys or wallets to help locate them if they're lost or misplaced. However, finding an AirTag inside your car can be challenging due to its size and the vast interior space of most vehicles. This guide will provide you with a series of straightforward steps and helpful tips to assist you in locating an AirTag within your car efficiently. **Materials Needed:** 1. iPhone or iPad running iOS 14.5 or later 2. An active Apple ID account linked to the device 3. The 'Find My' app installed and set up 4. Bluetooth enabled on the device 5. Optional: A friend or family member to assist 6. Optional: Additional tools such as flashlights or mirrors may also prove useful depending on the car model **Instructions:** **Step 1: Open the Find My App** On your compatible iPhone or iPad, open the preinstalled 'Find My' application. It is usually located on the home screen but can also be found via Spotlight Search by swiping down from any screen. The icon has three blue bubbles arranged in a circular pattern against a white background. *This step initiates the process of connecting to the AirTag.* **Step 2: Access Devices Tab** Once opened, tap on the 'Devices' tab at the bottom center part of the display. Here, all devices associated with your Apple ID should appear. Scroll through this list until you find the missing AirTag. *By selecting the Devices tab, we narrow our focus onto the item we wish to track - the AirTag.* **Step 3: Select Missing AirTag** Tap on the name of the missing AirTag. After selection, four options will populate under the title 'Actions'. These include Directions (which opens Maps), Play Sound, Notify When Found, and Lost Mode. For now, select 'Play Sound.' *Selecting 'Play Sound' triggers the AirTag to emit a loud chirping noise making it easier to hear and thus find.* **Step 4: Listen Carefully & Narrow Down Location** Start listening carefully for the sound once initiated. Begin searching near where you last remembered having the item or areas you frequent often when entering or exiting the vehicle. Keep moving around systematically covering potential hiding spots such as beneath seats, glove compartment, door pockets, seat cracks, etc. *Listening closely allows us to pinpoint the exact location based on proximity since AirTags utilize directional audio technology.* **Key Tips & Guidelines:** * If there are multiple people in the car during the search, have one person navigate using their phone while another moves objects aside or checks different locations. * Use additional light sources like flashlights to illuminate dark corners or crevices. * Utilize reflective surfaces or secondary mirrors to see hard-to-reach places. * Remove removable parts like floor mats, seat covers, etc., for better accessibility. * Patience is crucial; don't rush through spaces without thoroughly inspecting them first. * Turn off the engine and other noisy elements in the car to ensure optimal hearing conditions. Follow these simple yet effective steps meticulously, utilizing provided key tips and guidelines. With patience and persistence, you'll surely find your elusive AirTag hidden somewhere within your car! You can put the pan on it and play. Now I'm going to get something to eat. One for the little boy who lives down the lane. How Porcupine Got Quills Long ago, when the world was young, porcupines had no quills. One day, when Porcupine was in the woods, Bear came along and wanted to eat him. But, Porcupine climbed to the top of a tree and was safe. The next day, when Porcupine was under a hawthorn tree, he noticed how the thorns pricked him. He had an idea. He broke off some of the branches of the hawthorn and put them on his back. Then, he went into the woods to wait for Bear. When Bear sprang on Porcupine, the little animal just curled himself up in a ball. Bear had to go away, for the thorns pricked him too much. Iktomi saw what happened. He called Porcupine to him and asked, "How did you know that trick?" "I am always in danger when Bear comes along," replied Porcupine. "When I saw those thorns, I thought I would use them." So Iktomi took some branches from the hawthorn tree and peeled off the bark until they were white. Then he put some clay on the back of Porcupine, stuck the thorns in it and made the whole a part of his skin. "Now go into the woods," said Iktomi. Porcupine obeyed, and Iktomi hid himself behind a tree. Soon Wolf came along. He sprang on Porcupine and then ran away, howling. Bear came along, but he did not get near Porcupine. He was afraid of those thorns. That is why all porcupines have quills today! World English Institute JACOB & ESAU While Rebekah was pregnant with twins, the babies inside her struggled with each other. Rebekah prayed to the Lord and said, "Why is this happening to me?" The Lord said to Rebekah, "Two nations are in your body. The rulers of two families will be born from you. And they will be divided. One son will be stronger than the other. The older son will serve the younger." Isaac was sixty years old when his wife Rebekah gave birth to twin boys. The first baby was red. His skin was like a hairy robe, so he was named Esau, meaning "hairy." When the second baby was born, he was holding tightly to Esau's heel. So that baby was named Jacob, meaning "heel grabber." The name Jacob also means "tricky." When the boys grew up, Esau became a very great hunter. He loved to be in the fields. Jacob was a quiet man who stayed in his tent. Isaac loved Esau because he liked to eat the animals Esau killed. But Rebekah loved Jacob. ESAU SELLS HIS BIRTHRIGHT One time Esau came back from hunting. He was tired and weak from hunger. Jacob was boiling a pot of lentils. Esau said to Jacob, "I am weak with hunger. Let me have some of those red beans." Jacob replied, "You must sell me your rights as the firstborn son today." Esau said, "I am almost dead with hunger. If I die, all my father's wealth will not help me. So I will give you my share." Jacob said, "First, promise me that you will give your share to me." (As the oldest son, Esau would receive a double share of his father's property and would become the leader of the family when Isaac died.) So Esau made a promise. Then Jacob gave Esau bread and food. Esau ate and drank and then left. So Esau showed that he did not care about his rights as the firstborn son. JACOB GETS THE BLESSING OF THE FIRSTBORN When Isaac was old, his eyes were not good. Isaac could not see clearly. One day he called Esau to him. Isaac said, "Son!" Esau answered, "Here I am." Isaac said, "I am old. Maybe I will die soon! So take your bow and arrows and go hunting. Kill an animal for me to eat. Prepare the food that I love. Bring it to me, and I will eat. Then I will bless you before I die." So Esau went hunting. Rebekah was listening when Isaac told those things to his son Esau. Rebekah called her son Jacob and told him what she had heard. Rebekah said to Jacob, "Listen son, and do what I tell you. Go out to our flock and bring me two young goats. I will prepare them the way your father loves them. Then you will carry the food to your father, and he will bless you before he dies." Jacob did as his mother said. Rebekah cooked the goats in the special way that Isaac loved. Then Rebekah took the clothes that Esau loved to wear and put those clothes on the younger son Jacob. Rebekah took the skins of the goats and put them on Jacob's hands and on his neck. Then Rebekah got the food that she had cooked and gave it to Jacob. Jacob went in to his father and said, "Father." His father said, "Yes, son. Who are you?" Jacob said, "I am Esau, your first son. I have done the things you told me. Now sit up and eat the animals that I hunted for you. Then you can bless me." Isaac said, "Come near to me so that I can feel you, then I will know if you are really my son Esau." So Jacob went to Isaac his father. Isaac felt him and said, "Your voice sounds like Jacob's voice, but your arms are hairy like the arms of Esau." Then Isaac said, "Son, come near and kiss me." So Jacob went to his father and kissed him. Isaac smelled Esau's clothes and blessed him saying, "May the Lord give you plenty of rain so that you will have great harvests. May nations bow down to you. Your mother's sons will bow down and obey you." When Esau came home from hunting and found out what had happened, he became very angry and bitter. Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing and decided to kill him. So Jacob left Beersheba and went to Haran. JACOB DREAMS ABOUT A LADDER While Jacob was traveling, he stopped for the night because the sun had set. Jacob found a rock and laid his head on it to sleep. During the night Jacob dreamed there was a ladder that reached from heaven to earth. Jacob saw the angels of God going up and down the ladder. And then he saw the Lord standing by the ladder. The Lord said, "I am the Lord, the God of your grandfather Abraham. I am the God of Isaac. I will give this land to you and to your children. You will have many, many descendants, like the many pieces of dust on earth. All the families on earth will be blessed because of you and your descendants." Then Jacob woke from his sleep and said, "I know that the Lord is in this place. This is the house of God. This is the gate to heaven." Jacob rose very early in the morning. Jacob took the rock he had slept on and set it up on its edge. Then he poured oil on the rock. In this way, he made the rock a memorial to God. Then Jacob named that place Bethel, meaning "house of God." Jacob made an agreement with God that day. God promised Jacob, "I will be with you, and I will protect you every place you go. And I will bring you back to this land." JACOB MARRIES RACHEL Jacob continued his trip. Finally, he came to Haran in Mesopotamia. There, Jacob met his Uncle Laban's daughter Rachel and fell in love with her. So Jacob stayed with Laban a month. One day Laban said to Jacob, "It is not right for you to continue working for me without pay. You are a relative, not a slave. What should I pay you?" Laban had two daughters. The older was Leah and the younger was Rachel. Rachel was beautiful, and Jacob loved her. Jacob said to Laban, "I will work seven years for you if you will allow me to marry your daughter Rachel." Laban said, "It would be better for her to marry you than someone else. So stay with me." So Jacob worked for Laban seven years. But it seemed like a very short time because he loved Rachel very much. After seven years Jacob said to Laban, "Give me Rachel so that I can marry her. My time of work for you is finished." LABAN TRICKS JACOB So Laban gave a party for all the people in that place. That night, Laban tricked Jacob and brought his daughter Leah to Jacob. Jacob and Leah had sexual relations together. In the morning Jacob saw that it was Leah he had slept with. Jacob said to Laban, "You have tricked me. I worked hard for you so that I could marry Rachel. Why did you trick me?" Laban said, "In our country we do not allow the younger daughter to marry before the older daughter. But continue for the full week of the marriage ceremony, and I will also give you Rachel to marry. But you must serve me another seven years." So Jacob did this and finished the week. Then Laban gave him his daughter Rachel, and Jacob had sexual relations with Rachel also. Jacob loved Rachel more than Leah. GOD CHANGES JACOB'S NAME TO ISRAEL In all, Jacob worked fourteen years to pay for his wives Leah and Rachel. Then Jacob worked six more years for hire. During this time Jacob had eleven sons and one daughter by his two wives and their maids Zilpah and Bilhah. His sons' names were Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Dan, Naphtali, Gad, Asher, Issachar, Zebulun, and Joseph. The daughter's name was Dinah. After living in Haran twenty years, Jacob took his family and all his wealth and returned to the land of Canaan. There, God met him beside the Jabbok River. God struggled with Jacob during the night and changed his name to Israel, meaning "one who fights with God." Upon arriving at Ephrath (Bethlehem), Rachel died while giving birth to Benjamin. Rachel was buried there, and Jacob put a special rock on her grave to honor her. Each of Jacob's sons became the head of a very large family. After a period of time, these families became the nation of Israel. WHAT CAN WE LEARN FROM THIS STORY? 1. God has the right to rule. Before Jacob and Esau were born, and before they had done either good or bad, God chose Jacob over Esau. God is able to look ahead and see the future. Perhaps that is why God chose Jacob. He could see that Esau would be more concerned about his hunger than about spiritual matters. We don't know why God favored Jacob over Esau. We only know that God has the right to do whatever he wants to do. He is the potter and we are the clay. The clay does not say to the potter, "Why have you molded me like this?" 2. People can change. Jacob had some very bad faults. He lied. He cheated. He stole things that belonged to others. More than once he deceived members of his own family. But Jacob changed. Jacob learned what it is like to be tricked. Jacob suffered heartaches and disappointments within his own family, and these painful experiences helped him grow. Slowly God worked through every tragedy to change Jacob into a better person. And Jacob developed a relationship with God. When you look into the mirror, what do you see? Do you see someone with very bad faults? Someone who lies, cheats, steals, and deceives others? If so, don't despair. Don't give up on yourself. God is not through with you. The God who worked on Jacob is also working on us. Although Jacob was tricky when he was young, God was able to change him into a better person. The God who changed Jacob's name to "Israel" can also change us. 3. God points ahead to a Savior. In the Garden of Eden, God predicted that a descendant of Eve would crush Satan's head. Then, God promised that all families on earth would be blessed by a descendant of Abraham. Then God made an agreement with Isaac and Jacob and promised that the world would be blessed through them. Step by step God has pointed more clearly to the coming of that special Someone. The coming One would defeat Satan and bless the world. He would be a descendant of woman, of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob. God's picture of the coming One will become even clearer as his plan of salvation continues to unfold. A blog about creating art with children and adults...and what we learn along the way. Search Creating Art Monday, November 8, 2010 Kindergarteners learned about the art of Joan Miro. His work lends itself so well to elementary art and children seem to really respond to Miro's style. We talked about the way Miro used symbols instead of making his work look "real". Each student saw something different in his work, but, to my surprise, there was always one student in each class who thought the above picture looked like a bumble bee! I gave each of them a sheet of 12" x 18" tan construction paper and a big, fat, black crayon. I told them to stretch their arms out so that their drawing hands rested at the top of their papers. Then, with their eyes closed, to draw a big circle. I didn't want them to look at their papers, because it seems when they do, they think so hard about making the "perfect" circle, their circles come out tight and small. I wanted them to be very organic shapes, like Miro's. Next, they were instructed to draw a shape for their body and then add arms and legs- as many as they wanted. Some students did more than one figure on their paper. We observed the various lines and shapes Miro made in his work, and I instructed the students to decorate their papers with these symbols, or to make up their own. Students then painted inside their figures with black tempera paint. In our next art class, students added details on top of their figures with white crayons, and further embellished their art work with chalk pastels and a few spritz's of watercolor spray. I told the students I thought Miro was smiling on us and that he would be really proud of their work! Regarding publications, there are two of major significance. The code breakers in World War I didn't have supercomputers to do their work for them; they had to think for themselves. In today's modern world it's simple to write a script that does the work for you, but do you truly understand the script itself? Sure, you know how it works, what it does. But... take away the computer, could you still do that script? In this article I'm going to show you how to decrypt a basic sentence that has been run through the XECryption Algorithm, the same encryption method used in Realistic Mission 6. All you're going to need is a pen, a notepad, a calculator, and a hell of a lot of patience. For our example I've encrypted a small sentence, a little note to myself: - Code: Select all Now stop right there, because if you stare at it too long your head will begin to hurt. In its present state, it would be far too easy to get mixed up. Remember: The biggest obstacle in front of you is you. Human beings are by nature flawed and contain mistakes. Learn to accept them, think like a machine. If you know how the XECryption algorithm works, you know that each character is divided into three three digit numbers separated by periods, i.e.: x=.yyy.yyy.yyy Well if we know that, we can use that to our advantage. The first step is to break up the message, make it more simply for us to read. We'll divide it into each character, one at a time. After doing this, it'll look like this: Now that our message is at least slightly legible, we have a chance. The XECryption method, while complicated in its encryption, is quite simple in its decryption. You add all three numbers together, subtract them by the password key, and you get the ASCII version of your character. Of course, I'm not about to tell you my password, that would be too easy. But we'll deal with that later, for now let's go on the next step. The second step in decryption is going to be adding all three numbers together. This is why you brought your calculator. You might not need it, but I didn't get the best grades in high school, so I sure do. After adding the three numbers we should get something like this: Still a lot of numbers, but at least the size of our message is a third of what it used to be, and that's a lot more manageable. Now, the way we would get our final message would be to subtract the password key from our new numbers, but we don't know the password do we? Well, where there's a will there's a way. If you're new to the cryptography scene, let me recommend some of Edgar Allen Poe's works, the man was a genius when it came to this. So, what's the most common character used in any message? In English, the letter which most frequently occurs is e. Afterward, the succession runs thus: a o i d h n r s t u y c f g l m w b k p q x z. -Edgar Allen Poe Now, Poe didn't have a computer, so in modern times you need to add something. Even more common than the letter e is the space character, which will occur in any message more often than any other character. So, the question arises: What number pops up most frequently in my message? The answer is 783. Well, you can't be 100% sure, you can never be 100% sure, but 783 has the highest probability of being the space character. A quick lookup of ASCII will tell us that the space character is assigned the number 32. So, what's 783-32? 751. Now we have our password key: 751. After this it's smooth sailing, simply subtract 751 from each number, one at a time, and you'll get the decrypted message: - Code: Select all Don't forget to take out the trash man. Well, after the two hours it took me to decrypt that, the trash guy has already come and gone. But hey, I'll get him next time, right? In the world of "hacking", "cracking", "phreaking", or whatever you want to call it, we often take our computers for granted. Once you've done the script yourself, you'll understand what is truly going on behind the scenes, and it'll give you a new outlook on how the computers work. With this knowledge, you'll have a new perspective when making your scripts. and Cryptography? Logic, Common Sense, And Numbers By Lewis E. Love 1. She kept painting and finally made a picture her parents liked. The rapidity with which the wounded recovered was marvellous-- a result due, I suppose, to their having lived almost entirely on vegetables and milk, and always being out in the open air. Although there was much rejoicing on our return, there were also laments, because some of our bravest young men had been killed, and their relatives showed just as much regret at their loss as do the English when they lose a friend. The scenes through which I had passed during my residence among these Caffres, had produced upon me the same effects as though I had lived three or four times as long. Although I was at an age when English boys are engrossed with tops, hoops, and marbles, and look upon a performance at football or cricket as something to make a boy famous, yet I had trapped a leopard, had killed an elephant and had now been engaged in a desperate fight and had aided to win the victory. These stern realities had aged me, and I felt confidence now both in myself and in the weapons I could use: I sat in the circle with the warriors, and had, after the battle, spoken before the assembled people. I got another one for you if you want it. Cats only mate when the queen is "in heat". Heat periods occur about every two weeks and last 4 to 6 days. Mating in cats is a spectacular event. Several toms may be attracted to a queen in heat. The males will fight over her, and the victor wins the right to mate. At first, the female will reject the male, but eventually the female will allow the male to mate. The female will utter a loud yowl as the male pulls out of her. This is because a male cat's penis has a band of about 120-150 backwards-pointing spines, which are about one millimeter long. When the penis is withdrawn, the spines rake the walls of the female's vagina, which is a trigger for ovulation. After mating, the female will wash her vulva thoroughly. If a male attempts to breed with her at this point, the female will attack him. After about 20 to 30 minutes. once the female is finished grooming, the cycle will repeat. haha, can you give the baby a kiss? Title: How to Hold a Controller Using the Claw Method The claw grip is a popular technique used by gamers, especially those who play first-person shooter (FPS) games, fighting games, or any game that requires quick and precise movements. This method allows for faster response times and more comfortable gaming sessions. Here's our step-by-step guide on how to hold a controller using the claw method. ### Step 1: Familiarize Yourself with Your Controller Before attempting the claw grip, familiarize yourself with your console's controller layout. For this tutorial, we will be using a PlayStation DualShock 4 as an example. Make sure you know where all the buttons are located and their functions. **Key Tip:** Understanding your controller is crucial because misplacing fingers could lead to discomfort or even injury during extended gaming periods. ### Step 2: Position Your Index Fingers Correctly Place your middle and ring fingers on the R2 and L2 triggers respectively. These two fingers should rest naturally on these buttons without exerting pressure. They will primarily control actions like shooting, jumping, or interacting with objects depending on the game. **Key Guideline:** The goal here is to have easy access to both trigger buttons while keeping other fingers free for additional controls. ### Step 3: Place Your Thumbs Properly Your thumbs should sit comfortably over the analog sticks. Use your left thumb to maneuver your character around the map, aim at targets, etc., while your right thumb typically jumps, reloads, swaps weapons, or confirms selections. **Key Tip:** Keep your thumbs loose but firm enough for accurate movement and button presses. Overly tight grips can cause fatigue quickly. ### Step 4: Extend Your Index Fingers Upwards Now comes the unique part of the claw grip - extending your index fingers upwards so they curl slightly towards your knuckles. This positioning enables your index fingers to reach and press the upper row of face buttons (Triangle, Circle, Cross, Square). **Key Guideline:** It might feel unnatural initially, but practice makes perfect! Remember, maintaining flexibility in your hands reduces strain. ### Step 5: Adjust According To Comfort And Gameplay Demands Depending on what game you're playing, sometimes adjustments may need to be made mid-game. You might find certain situations requiring different finger positions. Always listen to your body; if something starts hurting, take a break and readjust. **Key Tip:** Avoid locking your joints or tensing muscles excessively. Stay relaxed yet focused. In conclusion, mastering the claw method takes time and patience. Don't rush into it expecting immediate results. Gradually incorporate this grip into your gaming routine until it becomes second nature. As always, remember to balance gaming time with breaks to prevent hand injuries. Happy gaming! It's important to approach this situation with respect and discretion to avoid misunderstandings or hurt feelings. This tutorial will provide you with several methods to find out if a girl is single, while maintaining her dignity and your own integrity. Step 1: Observe Her Social Media Presence One of the easiest ways to find out if a girl is single is by checking her social media profiles, such as Facebook, Instagram, or Twitter. Look for signs like the absence of a relationship status, no posts about a significant other, or lack of pictures together. However, keep in mind that some people choose not to share their relationship status publicly. Therefore, this method may not always yield accurate results. Key Tip: Be cautious when using this method, as it involves invading her privacy. Make sure you have a valid reason for investigating and do so discreetly without drawing attention to yourself. Step 2: Engage in Conversation Another way to determine if a girl is single is through conversation. Start by discussing general topics like hobbies, interests, and relationships in general before moving onto more specific questions related to her personal life. Ask open-ended questions that allow her to share information voluntarily, rather than directly asking if she has a boyfriend. For example, you could ask, "What did you enjoy most about being single?" or "Have you ever been in a serious relationship?" Guideline: Pay close attention to her responses, body language, and tone during these conversations. She might indirectly reveal clues about her relationship status or comfort level talking about it. Avoid pressing too hard if she seems uncomfortable or unwilling to discuss the topic further. Step 3: Attend Social Events Together If possible, attend social events where she is present, such as parties, gatherings, or group activities. These settings offer opportunities to observe her interactions with others, including any male companions. Keep an eye out for signals that indicate she is taken, such as frequent public displays of affection, exchanging gifts, or referring to someone as her partner. Key Tip: Remember that attending social events should never feel like stalking or spying on her. Instead, focus on enjoying the event itself and let observations arise naturally within the context of normal social interaction. Step 4: Consult Mutual Friends Mutual friends can often serve as valuable sources of information regarding someone's relationship status. Approach them casually and ask about the girl in question, mentioning that you were wondering if she was seeing anyone. Be prepared for varying degrees of accuracy from different individuals, depending on how closely they know her and the nature of their relationship. Guideline: Always maintain confidentiality when seeking information from mutual friends. Respect their trust by keeping the discussion private and avoiding gossip or speculation. Step 5: Directly Ask Someone Who Knows Her Well If all else fails, consider approaching someone who knows her well (e.g., family member, close friend) and politely ask about her relationship status. Explain why you are interested in knowing and express gratitude for their assistance. While this method may provide direct answers, remember that relying solely on secondhand information carries inherent risks of miscommunication or misinformation. Conclusion: Finding out if a girl is single requires patience, tact, and observation skills. By following these steps, you increase the likelihood of discovering her relationship status while minimizing potential harm or embarrassment for both parties involved. Ultimately, remember that everyone deserves respect and understanding - regardless of their current romantic involvement. It involves putting yourself out there, meeting new people, and being open to the possibility of love. This guide will walk you through the process step-by-step, providing clear instructions and useful tips along the way. Join clubs, attend events, or volunteer for organizations related to your favorite pastimes. * Recognize your personal qualities: Be aware of your positive traits, such as empathy, humor, or resilience, as well as areas where growth is needed. Self-awareness allows you to present yourself authentically and improve your relationships. Key tip: Embrace vulnerability - sharing your thoughts, feelings, and experiences honestly creates deeper connections with potential partners. ### Step 2: Define What You Want in a Boyfriend Once you have a solid understanding of yourself, think about the characteristics you desire in a boyfriend. Guideline: Make a list of must-have qualities and nice-to-have attributes. Revisit this list periodically to reassess your priorities. ### Step 3: Improve Your Social Skills Effective communication and interpersonal skills are crucial when seeking a boyfriend. Nodding, smiling, and making relevant comments shows you value their input. * Ask open-ended questions: Encourage conversation by asking thought-provoking queries that cannot be answered with a simple yes or no. * Show empathy: Put yourself in the other person's shoes and respond with compassion and understanding. The highest surface part of a wave is called the crest, and the lowest part is the trough. The vertical distance between the crest and the trough is the wave height. The horizontal distance between two adjacent crests or troughs is known as the wavelength. Wave height is affected by wind speed, wind duration, or how long the wind blows, and fetch, which is the distance over water that the wind blows in a single direction. If wind speed is slow, only small waves result. If the wind speed is great but it only blows for a few minutes, no large waves will occur. Also, if strong winds blow for a long period of time but over a short fetch, no large waves form. Large waves occur only when all three factors combine (Duxbury, et al, 2002.) Time, Angles & Fractions - Interactive White Board Clock activity The clock activity covers three topics, the first can demonstrate how to tell the time using an analogue clock. The second mode uses the the clock hands as a way of learning angles. The third mode uses the clock as a way to help understand fractions.The clocked can be altered to change colors and its overall styling. Note in this activity different controls are shown depending on the current mode The controls above the mode selector are always displayed. At the top you will see a digital clock, below are three buttons. Click the 12 button to display in normal 12 hour format with AM/PM. Click 24 button to change to the 24 hour format. Often you won't want to display the time at all, click off and the digital clock will disappear. The clock can be used to display the current time (based on the clock of the computer you are using). Click the real time to toggle this feature. This is useful as children can observe how the clock changes throughout the day. For random time problems click the random button and the clock hands will spin to produce a random time. So this is great for lots of quick fire questions. When the clock is not showing the actual real time, then small handles appear at the end of the hands, these and dragged into position. Time mode controls. First select time mode, below are a set of controls. These can be used in time problems or to view how much time has elapsed when showing the actual real time. The first one displays the hours passed. The second one the minutes that have passed. Underneath reset button sets both back to zero. So a brief example of how to use them would be click random time. Now ask what the class what time will be in say 1hour and 25 minutes. To demonstrate this click reset, so both minutes and hours are zero. Now drag the clock hand forward until the hours is 1 and the minutes is 25. The clock now displays the answer to the initial problem. Clocks have always been a useful way to teach about angles. This clock has angle measurements built in. First set the mode to angles, to get the control set. In angles mode you will now see a protractor button click it to toggle the display of the protractor. Next is the auto toggle button, click to turn it on and you will notice the protractor lines up automatically to measure the angle. Move the clock hands and the protractor will follow. Below the protractor button is a slider use this to change the size of the protractor. When measuring angles the large clock hands can be made thin for better accuracy by clicking thin. To display the angle click Ang, and click Arc to display the angle arc. When dragging the minute hand it currently snaps to the nearest minute, turn this feature off by clicking snap. Finally the type of angle can be changed using the select control. So you can choose the angle between the hour hand and minute hand, both clockwise and anti-clockwise. Or measure the angle of the second hand from 12 o'clock. This works very well with the realtime setting as children can see a full angle of 360 turned every minute. This helps learning of key angles such as 0 , 90 , 270 etc. Also conversions such as 30 secs * 6 = 180 Select fraction on the mode control to use the clock to teach fractions. Time statements such as 'a quarter past', 'half past' and 'a quarter to' obviously are linked to fraction. In the fraction mode a circle sector is painted from 12 o'clock to the minute hand position. Two large fractions are displayed the fraction of an hour shows the unsimplified fraction of minutes 60. The simplified fraction is the same fraction cancelled down when possible. The simple button toggles removal of the hour and second hands to give a simplified view. You can also click thin to toggle the thinner clock hands. The clock is divided according to the divider lines control. The default setting is auto, this divides the clock according to the fraction displayed. Essentially the clock is divided depending on the simplified fraction. However you can turn off auto mode and divide the clock according to the number selected. Percentages can also be shown by clicking to toggle the percentage display. Styling the Clock Select the style mode to access controls. So for example to change the colour of the numbers, click numbers followed by a colour from the colourpicker. To remove the numbers completely click 'X' in the colour picker. You can do this with other parts of the clock too, which obviously can make reading the clock more difficult. Further lessons on the telling the time involve comparison of two different times the two clocks activity is excellent in this regard and can also generate time questions. To expand on the angles try Basic angles which can be used to examine types of angles such as acute,obtuse and reflex. It can also so be used to create many different types of random angle problems. Having seen a circular fraction in this activity, the interactive fraction wall can also be used for further teaching of fractions. Factoring the Difference of Two Perfect Squares Factorizing mathematical expressions is an essential skill in algebra and higher mathematics. One common type of factoring is that of the difference between two perfect squares. This article will guide you through this process with clear explanations at every step, as well as providing some useful tips along the way. By following these steps, you'll be able to factorize similar expressions confidently and accurately. ### Understanding the Basics A perfect square is a number obtained by squaring a whole number (e.g., 4 = 2^2, 9 = 3^2). The difference between two perfect squares can be expressed as: a - b where 'a' and 'b' are integers, and 'a' > 'b'. Our goal here is to express the above expression as a product of simpler factors rather than leaving it as a difference between two squared terms. Key Tip: Remembering the formula for factoring differences of perfect squares saves time and reduces errors. It's always: a - b = (a + b)(a - b) Now let's move onto the actual process of factoring using this formula. ### Step 1: Identify the Square Terms Look at your equation and identify which parts represent the squares ('a' and 'b'). For example, if given the equation 64x - 9y , immediately recognize that 64x represents 'a ', since 8x * 8x = 64x . Similarly, notice that 9y represents 'b '. ### Step 2: Extract 'a' and 'b' from their Respective Squares Next, determine what numbers multiplied together give you your 'a ' and 'b ' values. Here, we see that 'a' equals 8x and 'b' equals 3y because when they are individually squared, they equal our original square terms. ### Step 3: Apply the Formula GAR would you like to see something else? [PLA attempts to connect the pop beads] Well she's just standing there I guess. Well he likes to pick up books anyway... [as Hal turns pages of the book] Hello young explorers! Today, let's learn about curves and shortest distances in different kinds of spaces. You might think that the shortest distance between two points is always a straight line, like when you walk diagonally across a square park to get to your friend on the other side. But in some special types of spaces, things can get a little more interesting! First, imagine a flat sheet of paper - this is a 2D Euclidean space. Now draw a curve or "path" connecting any two points on it. To find the length of this path, you would measure the distance along the curve from one point to another. If the curve doesn't bend too much and goes directly from one point to another without backtracking, then that curve could very well be the shortest path between those two points. We call these paths "geodesics." So, yes, on a flat sheet of paper, a straight line is indeed a geodesic because it represents the shortest distance between two points. Now, let's make things fascinatingly curvy! Imagine a balloon with a wiggly road drawn onto its surface, connecting two cities. This time, walking along the wiggly road does NOT give us the shortest distance between the start and endpoints. Instead, if you were to take a string and lay it down tautly over the bumpy terrain, keeping both ends attached to the starting and ending cities, THAT path would represent the shortest distance between them. On the balloon's surface, we again call this special path a geodesic. However, notice that unlike on the flat sheet of paper, here the geodesic isn't a straight line anymore but rather follows the shape of the balloon due to its curved nature. To sum up: Geodesics are like the shortest paths between points in various spaces, whether they're flat or curved. Sometimes they appear as straight lines, while other times they follow the contours of their environment. Just remember that no matter where you go exploring, there may be unique rules governing the shortest routes around you! Title: How to Have a Buddhist Girlfriend While Practicing Another Religion Introduction: Having a relationship with someone who follows a different religion can be challenging, especially if you are committed to your own faith. This is particularly true when it comes to dating a Buddhist girl while practicing another religion yourself. However, with understanding, respect, and open communication, such relationships can thrive. Here's a comprehensive guide on how to have a harmonious relationship with a Buddhist girlfriend while maintaining your own religious beliefs. Step 1: Understand Basic Buddhism Before starting any interfaith relationship, it's crucial to understand your partner's belief system. Familiarize yourself with basic Buddhist concepts like The Four Noble Truths, The Eightfold Path, Karma, Rebirth, and Nirvana. This will help you appreciate her perspective better and avoid misunderstandings about her practices or beliefs. Key Tip: Remember that Buddhism emphasizes personal spiritual development leading to enlightenment rather than conversion. Therefore, she may not expect you to convert but would appreciate your efforts to comprehend her faith. Step 2: Learn About Different Branches of Buddhism There are three main branches of Buddhism - Theravada, Mahayana, and Vajrayana (Tibetan Buddhism). Each branch has unique traditions, rituals, and philosophies. Knowing which one your girlfriend follows will give you deeper insights into her specific practices and beliefs. Guideline: Show genuine interest in learning more about her particular tradition within Buddhism without being pushy or judgmental. Step 3: Respect Her Beliefs and Practices Respect is vital in an interfaith relationship. Even though you might disagree with certain aspects of Buddhism, showing reverence towards your girlfriend's beliefs and practices shows maturity and consideration. Key Tips: - Attend temple visits or meditation sessions with her if invited. - Listen attentively when she talks about her faith. - Refrain from criticizing or mocking her beliefs. Step 4: Share Your Own Beliefs Openly Just as you make an effort to understand hers, encourage her to learn about yours too. Explain your customs, festivals, prayers, values, etc., honestly and patiently. Avoid using this opportunity to convince her of your faith's superiority; instead, focus on sharing your experiences and feelings. Step 5: Discuss Potential Conflicts Early On It's essential to discuss potential areas of conflict early in the relationship. For instance, talk about how you both plan to handle issues related to child-rearing, holidays, dietary restrictions, etc. Having these discussions openly reduces future disagreements and fosters mutual understanding. Step 6: Practice Compassion and Empathy Buddhism highly values compassion and empathy. By incorporating these qualities into your interactions, you create a supportive environment where differences are appreciated rather than judged. Step 7: Seek Guidance When Needed If complex issues arise, consider seeking advice from religious leaders, therapists, or counselors trained in interfaith relations. They can provide valuable guidance based on their expertise and experience dealing with similar situations. Conclusion: A successful interfaith relationship requires patience, compromise, understanding, and most importantly, love. By following the steps outlined above, you can build a strong bond with your Buddhist girlfriend while staying true to your own religious path. If he's gonna play at the park. Conducting a workshop is an excellent way to share knowledge, build skills, and foster collaboration among participants. This comprehensive guide will take you through the process of planning, organizing, and executing a successful workshop that meets your objectives and engages your audience. ### Step 1: Define Your Objectives and Target Audience Before you begin planning your workshop, clearly define your goals and specify the target audience. Knowing what you want to accomplish and who you are addressing will help you design effective content and choose appropriate activities. Key questions to consider include: * What do I hope to achieve with this workshop? (e.g., skill development, problem-solving, brainstorming) * Who is my intended audience? (Consider factors like job roles, experience level, industry.) * How many people am I expecting to attend? Defining these aspects early on ensures that your workshop remains focused and relevant to its attendees' needs. ### Step 2: Choose a Suitable Topic and Format Select a topic that aligns with your objectives and appeals to your target audience. Consider current trends, challenges, or areas where there may be room for improvement within your industry or organization. The format should support your objectives; options can range from lectures and demonstrations to group discussions and hands-on exercises. Common formats include: * Lecture-style presentations * Panel discussions * Case studies * Group projects * Interactive workshops * Role-playing scenarios Mixing different formats keeps participants engaged while catering to various learning styles. ### Step 3: Allocate Time and Resources Determine the duration of your workshop based on the scope of your material and the complexity of the activities involved. A full day allows more time for in-depth exploration, but shorter sessions might better suit busy schedules or introductory topics. Estimate resource requirements such as presentation materials, equipment, venue hire, refreshments, and facilitators. Remember to factor in any costs associated with these resources when setting registration fees or seeking sponsorship. ### Step 4: Select a Venue Choose a suitable location for your workshop that accommodates your expected number of attendees comfortably. Key factors to consider include accessibility, parking facilities, public transportation links, and proximity to amenities. Ensure the space has adequate seating arrangements, lighting, ventilation, and audiovisual equipment if necessary. Check internet connectivity if your session relies heavily on digital tools or resources. Additionally, ensure that the venue adheres to local health and safety regulations due to COVID-19 pandemic restrictions. ### Step 5: Create a Detailed Agenda Plan out your workshop agenda meticulously, allocating sufficient time for each activity. Begin with an introduction to set expectations, establish ground rules, and encourage networking among participants. Schedule breaks throughout the day to maintain energy levels and prevent cognitive overload. Allow extra time for transitions between activities and unexpected delays. End with a conclusion summarizing key points and next steps, followed by a Q&A session or open discussion. Here's a sample outline: 1. Introduction (10 minutes) * Welcome and introductions * Overview of objectives and agenda * Icebreaker exercise 2. Session 1 (60 minutes) * Presentation or lecture Sodium (symbol Na, from the Latin name "natrium") is the chemical element number 11 in the periodic table of elements. It follows that its nucleus includes 11 protons, and 11 electrons orbit around it (according to the simplified model known as "Niels Bohr atom"). Even if many isotopes can be artificially made, all decay in a short time. As a result, all sodium found in nature (mainly in sea water) has the composition Na, meaning that the nucleus includes 12 neutrons. Title: How to Enable Tablet Mode in Windows 10 Windows 10 offers a special mode called "Tablet Mode" that transforms your desktop or laptop into a tablet-like interface. This can be particularly useful when you're using a touchscreen device or if you prefer a more simplified layout. Here is an extensive, wiki-style guide on enabling this feature. **Step 1: Access Settings** To begin, click on the Start button (the Windows logo located at the bottom left corner of your screen) or press the Windows key on your keyboard. From there, select the gear icon which represents 'Settings'. If you find difficulty locating it, right-click anywhere on the taskbar and choose 'Settings' from the context menu. Key Tip: The settings app provides centralized access to all customizable features of your Windows 10 system. It's divided into several categories, making it easy to navigate through different options. **Step 2: Navigate to System Category** Once inside the Setting app, look for the category labeled 'System'. Clicking on it will open up a new window with various subcategories listed on the left side panel. Guideline: Each category in the Settings app relates to specific functionalities of your system. 'System', being one of the broadest categories, contains settings related to display, notification, power & battery, etc. **Step 3: Select Tablet Mode Option** Scroll down until you see 'Tablet Mode' among the list of subcategories under 'System'. Clicking on it will take you to the main page where you can enable and configure Tablet Mode settings. Did You Know? Microsoft introduced Tablet Mode primarily for convertible laptops and tablets running Windows 10. However, even traditional desktops and laptops without touchscreens can utilize this feature for its streamlined user interface. **Step 4: Enable Tablet Mode** On the right pane of the 'Tablet Mode' settings page, you'll notice a switch labeled 'Make Windows more touch-friendly...'. Toggle this switch to 'On'. Immediately, your interface should change, reflecting the characteristics of Tablet Mode - such as larger tiles, full-screen apps, and easier navigation via touch inputs. Important Note: When enabled, applications will launch maximized and behave like they do on mobile devices. You can still snap two apps side by side, but multi-window multitasking becomes limited compared to standard Desktop Mode. **Step 5: Customize Additional Behaviors** Underneath the primary toggle, there are additional options allowing you to specify how Tablet Mode behaves when you sign in, connect or disconnect a secondary monitor, and dock your device. Explore these settings based on your preference and usage pattern. Trivia: These advanced settings allow users to tailor their experience according to their hardware setup and personal comfort. For instance, someone might want Tablet Mode automatically activated whenever they undock their Surface Pro from its keyboard base. **Step 6: Test Your New Setup** Finally, test out your newly configured Tablet Mode. Try resizing windows, opening multiple apps, and navigating solely via touch input (if available). Familiarize yourself with the changes so you know what to expect next time you enter Tablet Mode. Remember, practice makes perfect! Feel free to experiment with different configurations until you discover what works best for you. After all, that's the beauty of having a fully customizable operating system like Windows 10. [trying to get Hap within the camera view] Are you taking the baby's clothes off? Creating a Yelp account can be a great way to discover new businesses in your area, write reviews for places you've visited, and connect with other users who share similar interests. Here is an in-depth guide on how to create a personal Yelp account: Step 1: Go to the Yelp Homepage Visit <https://www.yelp.com/> using your preferred web browser. This will take you to the Yelp homepage where you can search for local businesses, read reviews, and access various features of the platform. Step 2: Click on 'Sign Up' On the top right corner of the page, click on the 'Sign Up' button. This action will redirect you to the registration form where you can enter your details. Step 3: Choose Your Preferred Sign-Up Method Yelp offers several sign-up methods such as via email, Facebook, or Google. Select your preferred method by clicking on one of the corresponding buttons. For this example, we will proceed with signing up via email. Step 4: Enter Your Email Address Type in your active email address in the designated field. Make sure that you provide a valid email since Yelp will send a verification link to complete the registration process. Step 5: Create a Password Invent a strong password containing letters, numbers, and symbols for better security. Follow any character requirements shown under the password input box. Confirm your chosen password by entering it again in the adjacent field. Step 6: Provide Personal Information Enter your first name, last name, and zip code in their respective fields. These are essential pieces of information needed for creating a unique profile within Yelp's community. Step 7: Read Privacy Policy & Terms of Service Before moving forward, ensure that you have read through both the Privacy Policy and Terms of Service. Understanding these documents ensures that you know what data Yelp collects from its users and how they utilize such information. Once satisfied, tick the checkbox indicating acknowledgment. Step 8: Verify Your Email Address Check your email inbox for the verification message sent by Yelp. If not found there, try checking your spam folder just in case. Open the email and click on the provided verification link. You may also copy the URL and paste it directly into your browser's address bar if necessary. Step 9: Set Up Your Profile After verifying your email, you'll be prompted to customize your public profile. Upload a suitable profile picture - ideally something recognizable like a headshot. Fill out sections related to your occupation, education level, and areas of interest. Completing these sections makes it easier for others to find common ground when connecting with you. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Use real names instead of pseudonyms for credibility purposes. * Refrain from sharing sensitive personal info during registration. * Always logout after using shared computers or devices. * Regularly update your profile settings to maintain privacy. * Don't hesitate to report suspicious activities or violations of community standards. By following these steps and keeping our recommended tips in mind, you should now successfully own a brand new Yelp account! Title: How to Care for Zebra Danios Introduction: Zebra danios (Danio rerio) are popular freshwater aquarium fish known for their distinctive black and white stripes. They are hardy, easy to care for, and can be kept in community tanks with other peaceful species. This tutorial will provide you with comprehensive information on how to care for zebra danios, ensuring they thrive in your home aquarium. Step 1: Set Up an Appropriate Aquarium * Desired Outcome: A well-cycled tank that meets the needs of zebra danios, promoting their health and longevity. * Explanation: Before adding any fish, set up an appropriate aquarium environment. For zebra danios, choose a tank of at least 10 gallons (38 L). Provide hiding spots using plants or decorations, as well as open swimming space. Install a good quality filter to maintain water quality. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Use a gravel substrate; sand may cause digestive issues if ingested accidentally. + Keep live aquatic plants like java ferns or anacharis for added oxygen and natural filtration. + Avoid sharp objects or rough surfaces that could damage their delicate fins. + Cycle the tank before introducing zebra danios by establishing beneficial bacteria through processes such as the addition of pure ammonia or fish food, monitoring nitrite levels until zero is reached, then maintaining low nitrate levels. Step 2: Maintain Ideal Water Conditions * Desired Outcome: Optimal temperature, pH level, and water hardness for zebra danios' health and comfort. * Explanation: Monitor and adjust water parameters regularly. Zebra danios prefer temperatures between 72 F - 79 F (22 C - 26 C), pH ranging from 6.5 to 8.0, and dGH values within 5-19. Regular testing ensures stable conditions essential for their overall wellbeing. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Perform weekly partial water changes (around 25%) to keep nutrient levels balanced and reduce waste buildup. + Utilize a reliable test kit to measure ammonia, nitrite, nitrate, pH, and KH. + Invest in a heater with thermostat control to stabilize tank temperature fluctuations. Step 3: Feed Them Properly * Desired Outcome: Balanced nutrition supporting growth, coloration, and vitality while preventing overfeeding. * Explanation: Offer high-quality commercial fish foods specifically formulated for omnivorous tropical fish. Supplement their diet with frozen or live foods like brine shrimp, bloodworms, or daphnia once or twice per week. Adjust feeding amounts based on visual cues-stop when fish exhibit signs of fullness. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Divide daily feedings into two portions given every twelve hours. + Soak flake food briefly to soften it prior to feeding. He showed his paper to his sister. "He has a hat, a scarf, and a carrot nose." "Wow, Max, that's very good!" Lily said. She smiled and clapped. "I made a penguin. He has a black and white coat, a yellow beak, and orange feet." They put their papers on the wall with tape. They also stuck some stickers of stars, snowflakes, and trees. Their room looked very pretty and cozy. "Let's check the thermometer, Lily," Max said. He pointed to the window. "Maybe it's cold enough to make real snowmen outside." They ran to the window and looked at the thermometer. It was a red stick with numbers and a line. The line was very low, near the bottom. "Brrr, it's very cold, Max," Lily said. She shivered and hugged her brother. "The thermometer says it's minus five." "Minus five? What does that mean?" Max asked. He tilted his head and frowned. "It means it's colder than zero," Lily said. She was very smart and organized. She liked to learn new things. "Zero is when water freezes and becomes ice. Minus means less than zero. So minus five is very, very cold." "Wow, Lily, you know a lot!" Max said. He was very impressed and proud of his sister. "Maybe we can go outside and play in the snow later. But first, let's have some hot chocolate and cookies. Mom said she made some for us." "Yay, hot chocolate and cookies!" Lily said. She nodded and smiled. "That sounds yummy and warm. Let's go, Max." They held hands and ran to the kitchen. [drops the ball in front of Lin so that he can see how to do this] Add It Up = It Equals Success Young children can learn basic math skills just by talking with you. Math skills help in many ways--from doing well in school as a child, to doing well as an adult with other responsibilities. Math is a part of our every day life, and we need to understand it. Comparing is an important math activity. Try using words like more/less, long/short, high/low, wide/narrow, tall/short, light/heavy. Your toddler can ask for "more," and can understand "less." Some children's books, like Dr. Seuss' Foot Book, also can help your child learn about these words. Words that help young children understand the order of things like first/middle/last, big/bigger/biggest, low/lower/lowest, can help your child understand more about how things can be arranged. Help your child put toys on a high or low shelf, or arrange shoes by sizes, biggest to smallest. Talking about time is another way to understand math ideas. Young children might not be able to tell time, or understand all the words about time, but their understanding begins with your words. Talk about the time of day as it goes on. Use the words "early" and "late" to describe the time of day. Put words together like morning and breakfast, lunch and afternoon to help your child understand. Say, "You woke up from your nap. Now it's afternoon." Chores are a perfect way to use math. They can be learning experiences for young children. Some great ideas to help with math understanding are: Don't leave it to chance. Even though you may naturally use math words in every day talk, it is important to use these words with young children. It lets you know when they don't understand something, and you can use more words as their understanding and meaning grows. Your words and playing with your child can make math fun. - setting the table - sorting laundry - putting toys away - cooking and measuring - planning the day Prepared by Marsha Hawley, Extension Educator, Family Life. Title: How to Get Rid of COVID-19 Videos Online Introduction: The ongoing global pandemic has led to an overwhelming amount of information related to COVID-19 being shared online. Unfortunately, this includes misinformation, hoaxes, and harmful content that can cause panic or confusion. One such issue is the spread of unwanted or false videos about COVID-19. This guide will help you understand how to identify these types of videos and take appropriate actions to get rid of them from your online platforms. Step 1: Identify Unwanted or False COVID-19 Videos Before reporting any video, make sure you have identified it as either unwanted or containing false information. Here are some signs to look out for: - Sensationalized headlines or descriptions unsupported by facts - Lack of credible sources or references - Contradictory claims when compared with trusted health organizations (e.g., World Health Organization, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention) - Emotional manipulation through fear, anger, or excitement - Requests for personal information or donations Key Tip: Always double-check information using reputable sources before sharing or acting upon it. Step 2: Reporting the Video on Social Media Platforms Each social media platform provides tools for users to report inappropriate content. Follow these steps based on which site the video appears on: For Facebook: 1. Click on the three dots at the top right corner of the post. 2. Select 'Find Support or Report Post.' 3. Choose the most suitable option describing why you're reporting the post, then click 'Send.' For Twitter: 1. Tap the arrow icon below the tweet. 2. Select 'Report Tweet.' 3. Choose the reason for reporting and follow further prompts. For YouTube: 1. Below the video player, click 'More' followed by 'Report.' 2. Provide additional details explaining why you're reporting the video. Guideline: Each platform may require different reasons for reporting; select those that closely match the issues mentioned in Step 1 above. Step 3: Contacting Site Administrators Directly If the video exists outside major social media networks, contact the website administrator directly via their provided email address or contact form. Explain clearly what the video contains, where it can be found, and why it should be removed. Remember to remain respectful during communication. Step 4: Reach Out to Fact-Checking Organizations Various fact-checking organizations exist globally, dedicated to debunking myths and misleading narratives. Some notable ones include Snopes, FactCheck.org, and PolitiFact. Submit the link to the questionable video along with your concerns so they might investigate and publicly discredit the video if proven false. Step 5: Spread Awareness About Verified Information Once you've successfully reported the video, share verified information regarding COVID-19 from reliable sources like the CDC, WHO, or local public health authorities to counterbalance the effects of the fake video. Educating others about accurate information plays a crucial role in combatting disinformation. Conclusion: Dealing with misleading or harmful videos requires vigilance and proactive action. By following these steps, individuals can contribute positively towards maintaining truthful and helpful discussions around COVID-19. Keep yourself informed using credible resources and continue promoting digital literacy among peers. oh, what shall we do at the park?
