And the little boy is laughing XXX, isn't he? = = = children/CHIDES_NA/NewmanRatner/18/5661AP.cha = = = Creating a Gravatar =================== A Gravatar (Globally Recognized Avatar) is an image that represents you online, often used next to your name when commenting or posting on websites. This tutorial will guide you through creating your own personalized Gravatar. **Time Required:** 15-20 minutes ### **Step 1: Go to the Gravatar Website** Visit the official Gravatar website at <https://en.gravatar.com/>. This is where you'll create and manage your avatar. ### **Step 2: Sign Up or Log In** If you already have a WordPress.com account, click on the 'Log In' button and enter your credentials. If you don't have one yet, click on 'Sign Up', then choose 'I need an account'. Follow the prompts to sign up using your email address. **Key Tip**: Using the same email address as your other online accounts can help simplify things, since gravatars are linked to emails. ### **Step 3: Confirm Your Email Address** Check your email for a message from Gravatar with the subject line "[Gravatar] Activate Your Account". Click on the activation link provided in this email. ### **Step 4: Set Your Password** You'll be redirected back to the Gravatar site where you're asked to set a password for your new account. Make sure to choose something secure but memorable. ### **Step 5: Access Your Dashboard** After setting your password, log into your newly created Gravatar account. You'll land on your dashboard which shows any existing gravatars associated with different emails. ### **Step 6: Add An Email Address** To add a new email, look under 'My Account' section and click on 'Add A New E-mail Address'. Enter the email address you want to associate with your gravatar and hit 'Next'. ### **Step 7: Verify Your Email Address** Another verification email will be sent to your specified mailbox. Open it and click on the confirmation link. ### **Step 8: Upload Or Select An Image** Once verified, you'll be directed back to your dashboard. Hover over the empty square where your new gravatar should go and click 'add'. Now you can either upload a new photo or select one from previously uploaded ones. **Guideline**: For best results, use a square image with minimum dimensions of 200x200 pixels. ### **Step 9: Crop Your Image** After selecting or uploading, you'll be prompted to crop your image so it fits nicely within the circle shape of a gravatar. Drag the selection box to fit the part of the image you want visible, then press 'Crop Image'. ### **Step 10: Rate Your Avatar** Now you need to rate your avatar according to its content - G, PG, R, or X. Most users would stick to G or PG. ### **Step 11: Save Changes** Finish by clicking 'Save'. Your new gravatar is now live! **Key Tips**: - Use consistent gravatars across all your online profiles to build recognition. - Remember that changing your gravatar affects everywhere it appears, so think carefully before making alterations. By following these steps, you've successfully created a Gravatar, enhancing your digital presence and identity! See how that goes on top of there? Title: Change Your Primary Apple ID Phone Number on an iPhone **Disclaimer:** Before proceeding with this guide, ensure that you have access to your current Apple ID password and any trusted phone numbers or email addresses associated with your account. This process may require verification codes sent via SMS or automated calls. Step 1: Open Settings App ------------------------- On the home screen of your iPhone, find and tap on the gray gear icon labeled "Settings." The settings app is where all device configurations are managed, including managing your Apple ID. Step 2: Access Apple ID Menu ----------------------------- Scroll down until you see your name at the top banner. Tap on it which will take you directly to the Apple ID management page. If you're using iOS 10.2 or earlier, go to 'iCloud,' then select your Apple ID email address. Step 3: Navigate to Contact Information -------------------------------------- From the main menu under your name, choose 'Name, Phone Numbers, Email.' Here, you can view and edit contact details linked to your Apple ID. Step 4: Edit Current Phone Number --------------------------------- Under 'REACHABLE AT', locate the section titled 'Primary Phone'. To its right, there should be a circle arrow button inside a box - press this to begin editing. Replace the existing number with the new one you wish to set as primary. Key Tip: Make sure to enter the country code (+1 for USA) before entering the actual phone number. It must be formatted correctly (for example, +1 XXX-XXX-XXXX). Step 5: Save Changes -------------------- Once entered, click 'Done' on the keyboard. A prompt asking if you want to update your Apple ID with the new information appears. Select 'OK'. Step 6: Verify New Phone Number ------------------------------ Apple sends a verification code to the newly added phone number via text message or call. Retrieve this code from your device. Enter the received verification code into the provided field when asked. Guideline: Be careful during this step since inputting incorrect codes too many times might lock your account temporarily due to security reasons. Step 7: Confirm Successful Update ---------------------------------- After successfully verifying the new phone number, it becomes your primary contact method. You can confirm this by checking back under 'Primary Phone' within 'Name, Phone Numbers, Email'. Remember, changing your primary phone number impacts how you receive two-factor authentication codes related to your Apple ID. Always keep your personal information up-to-date and secure. Creating your own theme in Gmail can be a fun and rewarding way to personalize your email experience. By following these steps, you'll be able to create a unique and customized look for your Gmail account. Step 1: Open Gmail and click on the gear icon Locate the gear icon in the upper right-hand corner of your Gmail screen. Clicking on this icon will open a dropdown menu with various options. Step 2: Select "See all settings" From the dropdown menu, select "See all settings." This will take you to the main settings page for your Gmail account. Step 3: Go to the "Themes" tab Once you are on the settings page, locate the tabs at the top of the screen and click on "Themes." This is where you will be able to customize the appearance of your Gmail account. Step 4: Choose a base theme On the Themes tab, you will see a variety of pre-made themes that you can choose from as a starting point. These themes range from solid colors to more complex designs featuring landscapes or patterns. You can hover over each theme to get a preview of what it will look like. Once you have found a theme that you like, click on it to select it as your base theme. Step 5: Customize the background image (optional) If you would like to add a background image to your theme, click on the "Background image" option located below the list of pre-made themes. From here, you can upload an image from your computer or choose one from Google's library of background images. Keep in mind that if you choose a light colored image, it may make the text harder to read so consider using a darker color scheme. Step 6: Adjust the colors Next, adjust the colors of your theme by clicking on the "Color palette" button located next to the "Background image" option. Here, you can change the color of the navigation bar, links, and text. It is recommended to keep the contrast high between the background and the text to ensure readability. Step 7: Save changes Once you are satisfied with your new theme, click on the "Save Changes" button located at the bottom of the screen. A confirmation message will appear, letting you know that your changes have been saved. Key Tips & Guidelines: * When choosing a background image, try to avoid images that are too busy or distracting. Simple images work best and make sure they don't clash with the text color. * Make sure there is enough contrast between the background and the text color. Low contrast makes text hard to read and causes eye strain. * Consider accessibility when designing your theme, especially for people who are visually impaired. Use high contrast colors, large font sizes, and clear labels. * If you want to revert back to the default theme, simply go back to the Themes tab and select "Default" under the pre-made themes section. * Remember to save any important emails before changing your theme as some elements might temporarily disappear during the transition. By following these steps and keeping our key tips in mind, you should now have created your own unique theme in Gmail! Enjoy your newly designed email inbox. [puts a silver stick up against the washers stuck to the side of the base, positioned as a ramp leading from the side of the tower down to the table-top] Title: How to Cut a Bath Bomb in Half Introduction: Bath bombs are popular bath time products that fizz and dissolve when they come into contact with water. They can be found in various shapes, sizes, colors, and scents. If you're curious about what's inside or want to create smaller portions for multiple uses, cutting a bath bomb in half is an easy process. This article will guide you through the steps required to cut a bath bomb in half safely and effectively. Things You'll Need: - A whole bath bomb - A sharp knife (a kitchen knife or a craft knife) - A stable, flat surface - Optional: Gloves and safety goggles Step 1: Choose Your Knife Select a sharp knife suitable for the size and shape of your bath bomb. Kitchen knives work well for larger, rounder bath bombs while small craft knives might be more appropriate for intricately shaped ones. Make sure the blade is clean and dry before proceeding. Key Tip: Using a dull knife could cause the bath bomb to crumble or crack as opposed to slicing evenly. Therefore, always choose a sharp instrument. Step 2: Put On Protective Gear (Optional) Although optional, wearing gloves and safety goggles provides extra protection against any potential injuries from accidental cuts or flying debris during the cutting process. Step 3: Prepare a Stable Surface Find a sturdy, level surface where you can perform the task without worrying about damaging anything below. It should also provide enough space for both halves of the bath bomb once separated. Step 4: Position the Bath Bomb Place the bath bomb upright on the chosen surface. For spherical or cylindrical bath bombs, position them so their diameter runs parallel to the edge of your working area. This orientation allows for easier penetration and less chance of slippage during the incision. Step 5: Mark the Center Line Before making the actual cut, draw a line along the center point of the bath bomb using a marker pen or the tip of the knife itself. Press lightly to avoid damaging the outer layer but make sure the mark is visible enough to serve as guidance throughout the procedure. Key Guideline: Ensuring symmetry by dividing the bath bomb equally enhances its aesthetic appeal post-cutting. Step 6: Apply Even Pressure Holding the knife firmly at approximately a 45-degree angle, press downward slowly and steadily along the marked line until the knife has fully penetrated the bath bomb. Try maintaining equal pressure across the entire length of the cut to ensure an even split. Key Tip: Applying too much force risks breaking the knife or causing uneven division; hence slow and steady wins the race here. Step 7: Complete the Incision Continue applying gentle yet consistent pressure until you feel the knife reaching the opposite end of the bath bomb. At this stage, lift the handle slightly upwards while keeping the blade pressed downwards to widen the gap between the two halves. Step 8: Separate the Halves Gently twist and pull apart the two sections of the bath bomb. If resistance persists due to adhesion caused by moisture, allow the pieces to rest briefly before attempting separation again. And voila! There you have it - perfectly cut halves of your beloved bath bomb. Enjoy observing its inner beauty or share one half with someone special. Always remember to store the unused portion properly to preserve its effervescence for future use. No Adam you can't turn his head. What the heck do you have a boot on his head for? 8-9 lengthened and in a singing manner **Title: Understanding Prime Numbers and Multisets** Hello young learners! Today, we are going to explore the fascinating world of numbers, specifically focusing on something called "prime numbers." Have you heard of them before? Don't worry if you haven't - let me explain! Imagine counting one sheep, then another, and so on. The first few numbers you get are 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, and so on. A prime number is a special kind of number that cannot be divided evenly by any other number except itself and 1. So, among these initial numbers, 2, 3, and 5 are prime numbers because there is no whole number smaller than them that divides them exactly. Can you spot more prime numbers while continuing to count? Now, let us introduce another concept called a "multiset." In real life, imagine collecting different types of candies in a bag. You could have multiple quantities of each candy type, like having three red gummy bears, two blue lollipops, and four green jelly beans. Similarly, in mathematics, a multiset is a collection of items where repetition or multiplicity matters, just like our candy example. Each item in a multiset may appear several times, unlike regular sets where every element occurs precisely once. Here comes the interesting part: combining both concepts! Given a specific number N, your task is to discover the largest possible number M, which isn't necessarily a prime but acts as an upper limit for the prime factors of the numbers within a multiset. This means that none of the numbers in the multiset will contain prime factors larger than M. Additionally, you should always be able to select two distinct numbers X and Y from this multiset, ensuring their product results in a perfect square number. How would YOU approach solving this problem? Let's dive deeper into understanding the solution provided in the original text using easier language and relatable examples. Keep exploring, and soon enough, you'll uncover the secrets behind prime numbers and multisets! 'Cause the people on their trip with Rex Trailer get to have a limousine pick them up. You'll probably want to hide YouTube's captions if using these subtitles. - Welcome to the logarithm presentation. - Let me write down the word "logarithm" just because - it is another strange an unusual word, like "hypotenuse", - and it's good to at least see it once. - Let me get the pen tool working. - This is one of my most misspelled words. - I went to MIT and actually one of the a cappella groups there, - they were called the Logarhythms. - Like rhythm, like music. - But anyway, I'm digressing. - So what is a logarithm? - Well, the easiest way to explain what a logarithm is is - to have first-- I guess it's just to say it's the inverse of - taking the exponent of something. - Let me explain. - If I said that two to the third power-- well, we know that - from the exponent modules. - two the third power, well that's equal to eight. - And once again, this is a two, it's not a z. - two to the third power is eight, so it actually turns out that - log-- and log is short for the word logarithm. - Log base two of eight is equal to three. - I think when you look at that you're trying to say oh, - that's trying to make a little bit of sense. - What this says, if I were to ask you what log base two of - eight is, this says two to the what power is equal to eight? - So the answer to a logarithm-- you can say the answer to this - logarithm expression, or if you evaluate this logarithm - expression, you should get a number that is really the - exponent that you would have to raised two to to get eight. - And once again, that's three. - Let's do a couple more examples and I think you might get it. - If I were to say log-- what happened to my pen? - log base four of sixty-four is equal to x. - Another way of rewriting this exact equation is to say four to - the x power is equal to sixty-four. - Or another way to think about it, four to what - power is equal to sixty-four? - Well, we know that four to the third power is sixty-four. - So we know that in this case, this equals three. - So log base four of sixty-four is equal to three. - Let me do a bunch of more examples and I think the more - examples you see, it'll start to make some sense. - Logarithms are a simple idea, but I think they can get - confusing because they're the inverse of exponentiation, - which is sometimes itself, a confusing concept. - So what is log base ten of let's say, one million. - Put some commas here to make sure. - So this equals question mark. - Well, all we have to ask ourselves is ten to what power - is equal to one million. - And ten to any power is actually equal to one followed by the - power of-- if you say ten of the fifth power, that's equal - to one followed by five zero's. - So if we have one followed by six zero's this is the same thing - as ten to the sixth power. - So ten to the sixth power is equal to one million. - So since ten to the sixth power is equal to one million log base - ten of one million is equal to six. - Just remember, this six is an exponent that we raise ten - to to get the one million. - I know I'm saying this in a hundred different ways and - hopefully, one or two of these million different ways that I'm - explaining it actually will make sense. - Let's do some more. - Actually, I'll do even a slightly confusing one. - log base one / two of one / eight. - Let's say that that equals x. - So let's just remind ourselves, that's just - like saying one / two-- whoops. - one / two. - That's supposed to be parentheses. - To the x power is equal to one / eight. - Well, we know that one / two to the third power is equal to one / eight. - So log base one / two of one / eight is equal to three. - Let me do a bunch of more problems. - Actually, let me mix it up a little bit. - Let's say that log base x of twenty-seven is equal to three. - What's x? - Well, just like what we did before, this says that x to the - third power is equal to twenty-seven. - Or x is equal to the cubed root of twenty-seven. - And all that means is that there's some number times - itself three times that equals twenty-seven. - And I think at this point you know that that - number would be three. - x equals three. - So we could write log base three of twenty-seven is equal to three. - Let me think of another example. - I'm only doing relatively small numbers because I don't have - a calculator with me and I have to do them in my head. - So what is log-- let me think about this. - What is log base one hundred of one? - This is a trick problem. - So once again, let's just say that this is equal - to question mark. - So remember this is log base one hundred hundred of one. - So this says one hundred to the question mark power - is equal to one. - Well, what do we have to raise-- if we have any number - and we raise it to what power, when do we get one? - Well, if you remember from the exponent rules, or actually not - the exponent rules, from the exponent modules, anything to - the zero-th power is equal to one. - So we could say one hundred to the zero power equals one. - So we could say log base one hundred hundred of one is equal to zero - because one hundred to the zero-th power is equal to one. - Let me ask another question. - What if I were to ask you log, let's say base two of zero? - So what is that equal to? - Well, what I'm asking you, I'm saying two-- let's - say that equals x. - two to some power x is equal to zero. - So what is x? - Well, is there anything that I can raise two to - the power of to get zero? - So this is undefined. - Undefined or no solution. - There's no number that I can raise two to the - power of and get zero. - Similarly if I were to ask you log base three of - let's say, negative one. - And we're assuming we're dealing with the real numbers, - which are most of the numbers that I think at this point - you have dealt with. - There's nothing I can raise three three to the power of to - get a negative number, so this is undefined. - So as long as you have a positive base here, this - number, in order to be defined, has to be greater than-- well, - it has to be greater than or equal-- no. - It has to be greater than zero. - Not equal to. - It cannot be zero and it cannot be negative. - Let's do a couple more problems. - I think I have another minute and a half. - You're already prepared to do the level one logarithms module, - but let's do a couple of more. - What is log base eight-- I'm going to do a slightly - tricky one-- of one / sixty-four. - We know that log base eight of sixty-four would equal two, right? - Because eight squared is equal to sixty-four. - But eight to what power equals one / sixty-four? - Well, we learned from the negative exponent module that - that is equal to negative two. - If you remember, eight to the negative two power is the same - thing as one / eight to the two power. - eight squared, which is equal to one / sixty-four. - I'll leave this for you to think about. - When you take the inverse of whatever you're taking the - logarithm of, it turns the answer negative. - And we'll do a lot more logarithm problems and explore - a lot more of the properties of logarithms in future modules. - But I think you're ready at this point to do the level one - logarithm set of exercises. - See you in the next module. Adopting a pen name can be an exciting and creative process that allows writers to express themselves under a different identity. This separate persona can provide numerous benefits such as privacy, freedom from personal bias, and even marketing opportunities. Here is a comprehensive guide on adopting a pen name: **Step 1: Determine Your Reasons for Using a Pen Name** Before diving into choosing your new moniker, consider why you want to adopt one. Understanding your motivations will help shape the rest of the process. Some common reasons include maintaining privacy, separating professional and personal lives, escaping preconceived judgments based on real names, or simply finding a more memorable writing name. **Step 2: Brainstorm Potential Names** Start by listing out words, phrases, or ideas associated with your work. These could relate to genre, theme, character traits, settings, or any other relevant aspect of your writing. Then mix and match these elements until something feels right. You might also draw inspiration from favorite authors, historical figures, mythology, literature, etc. Remember, there are no rules here - let creativity flow! **Step 3: Check Availability Across Platforms** Once you have several options, check their availability across various platforms including social media sites (Twitter, Instagram, Facebook), websites, email providers, retailers like Amazon Kindle Direct Publishing, royalty collection agencies, writing communities, etc. Tools like Knowem, Namecheckr, or Google's search engine can assist in this task. Avoid names already taken or trademarked. **Step 4: Consider Legal Requirements** While using a pen name generally doesn't require legal documentation unless you plan to copyright works under said name, some countries may have specific laws regarding tax filing, contracts, royalties, etc., especially if income is generated through your writings. Consult local authorities about necessary procedures before proceeding further. **Step 5: Announce Your New Identity** After settling on a final choice, announce your new identity publicly via your existing networks, website, blog posts, newsletters, etc. Make sure to inform close friends and family too. Establishing yourself early on as your chosen pseudonym helps build credibility and recognition over time. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** - **Simplicity**: Opt for simple spellings and pronunciation to avoid confusion and make it easier for readers to find you online. - **Uniqueness**: Stand out from the crowd while still being easy to remember. A unique name enhances brandability and recall value. - **Relevance**: Align your pen name with your target audience and content type. For instance, a serious non-fiction author should probably avoid whimsical names. - **Avoid Sensationalism**: Steer clear of provocative or misleading names just to attract attention; authenticity matters most. - **Consistency**: Use your pen name consistently across all channels to strengthen your author identity and ensure discoverability. By following these steps and guidelines, you'll be well on your way to establishing a strong, recognizable presence under your adopted pen name. Happy Writing! 'Cause he will't let me use XXX. [Wal reaches for the bell and then drops toys on the floor] Whey' the ya want to do with it? Downloading songs onto your iPod is a great way to have your favorite tunes with you wherever you go. This tutorial will guide you through the process step-by-step using Apple's official software, iTunes. **Step 1: Install iTunes** Before you can download music onto your iPod, you need to install iTunes on your computer. Visit <https://www.apple.com/itunes/download/> and click 'Download Now'. Run the installation file once downloaded. Follow the prompts to complete the setup. Having the latest version ensures compatibility and optimal performance. **Step 2: Connect Your iPod to Your Computer** Use the USB cable provided with your iPod to connect it to an available port on your computer. Wait for your device to appear in iTunes (usually under 'Devices'). If it doesn't automatically show up, make sure your iPod is turned on and try reconnecting or changing ports. **Step 3: Sync Your iPod with iTunes** Synchronization allows iTunes to manage what content appears on your iPod. Click on your iPod icon within iTunes. Under the 'Summary' tab, check 'Automatically sync when this iPhone is connected'. You may also manually manage content by unchecking this box if preferred. **Key Tip**: Always ensure both devices are updated to their latest versions to avoid potential issues during synchronization. **Step 4: Organize Your Music Library** In order to download songs onto your iPod, they must first be part of your iTunes library. Arrange your music collection by adding files from various locations into one centralized location - your iTunes Media folder. To do so, select 'File'>'Add Folder to Library', then navigate to where your music resides. **Step 5: Choose Which Songs to Transfer** Navigate to the 'Music' section under 'Settings' after selecting your iPod in iTunes. Here, you can choose which playlists, artists, albums, genres, or individual tracks to transfer to your device. Select according to preference. **Step 6: Initiate The Sync Process** Once you've decided upon your selection, initiate the sync process either by clicking 'Apply' at the bottom right corner of the screen or by pressing the 'Sync' button at the lower right. Depending on the size of the transfer, this could take several minutes. **Guideline**: Avoid disconnecting your iPod while syncing as doing so might corrupt data or damage the device. **Step 7: Disconnect & Enjoy!** After the sync completes successfully, eject your iPod safely via iTunes before disconnecting physically. A message should pop up saying "iPod is safe to disconnect." Once done, locate your chosen songs in the 'Music' app on your iPod and enjoy! Remember, managing large libraries and multiple devices requires patience and organization but yields satisfying results. With these steps followed correctly, you now know how to download songs onto your iPod efficiently and effectively. What does mommy use this for at home? Logical consistency is a cornerstone of any acceptable theory. Philosophers who disagree with a theory will often try to find a logical contradiction in a theory. If they find a contradiction, this gives them a reason to reject that theory. If they do not find an inconsistency, the philosopher might show that the theory leads to a conclusion which is either unacceptable or ridiculous. Title: How to Do the "Walk the Dog" Trick with a Yo-Yo The "walk the dog" trick is one of the most iconic yo-yo tricks that even non-yoyoers recognize. Solving proportions is a crucial skill when studying similar polygons. The ratio of corresponding side lengths between similar polygons are equal and two equivalent ratios are a proportion. For solving proportions problems, we set up the proportions and solve for the missing side length - it will be a variable, or a variable expression. When you're talking about similarity, 2 figures that are similar, you're going to be using proportions. So just because we've moved on beyond Algebra doesn't mean that you can forget it. A ratio is just one relationship between two different numbers. A proportion is when you say that two ratios are equal to each other. And you'll be using these all throughout your similarity unit. So let's look at one quick example here. If I gave you this proportion, and I asked you to solve it for x, the way that you were taught back in grade school still applies. You could cross multiply. So we could say that x times 21, is equal to 7 times 18. So Math teachers cringe a little bit when they just say cross multiply but it will work for Geometry. So to solve this for x, we're going to divide both sides by 21. Now notice that I didn't multiply 7 times 18. I'm going to use factors to simplify here. So I'm going to write 21 as the product of 7 times 3. Now I can cancel out the sevens and see that I have 18 divided by 3. Which means x must be 6. There's multiple ways to solve proportions. But this way that you're shown will always work. Where you cross multiply and then you have to divide by whatever numbers multiplying your variable. Designing a blouse can be an exciting and rewarding experience. This tutorial will guide you through the process, from conceptualization to the final product. We'll cover everything you need to know, including gathering inspiration, choosing the right fabric, creating a pattern, sewing, and fitting. **Step 1: Gather Inspiration** Start by looking for inspiration. Browse fashion magazines, websites, or social media platforms like Pinterest and Instagram. Save or print out pictures of blouses that catch your eye. Pay attention to details such as necklines, sleeves, lengths, and embellishments. This will help you define what you like and dislike, enabling you to create a unique design. **Step 2: Sketch Your Design** Using your gathered inspirations, sketch your own design. Don't worry about perfection; this is just a rough idea. Include all necessary elements like collars, cuffs, buttons, pockets, etc. Remember to consider body types when designing - ensure your design flatters various shapes and sizes. **Step 3: Choose the Right Fabric** The choice of fabric significantly affects the look and feel of your blouse. Consider factors such as drape (how well the fabric hangs), weight, texture, and washability. For instance, lightweight fabrics with good drape are ideal for flowy designs, while stiffer materials suit structured styles better. Common choices include cotton, silk, linen, rayon, and polyester blends. Always pre-wash your chosen fabric to avoid shrinkage after completing your garment. **Step 4: Create a Pattern** There are two ways to get a pattern: buy one or draft your own. Buying a commercial pattern is easier if you're new to sewing, but it may not perfectly match your design. Drafting your own gives more control over the fit and style but requires knowledge of patternmaking techniques. There are many online resources available to learn these skills. Once you have a pattern, adjust it according to your measurements and design specifications. **Step 5: Cut Out the Fabric** Lay out your pattern pieces on your folded fabric, making sure to align grain lines properly. Use pattern weights or pins to hold them in place. Before cutting, double-check the placement of any design features requiring special consideration, like stripes or patterns. Carefully cut along the outline of each piece using sharp scissors or a rotary cutter. **Step 6: Sew the Blouse** Begin constructing your blouse following the order outlined in your pattern instructions. Start with darts, then move onto seams, facings, and finally, hems and closures. Take your time to ensure accurate seam allowances and neat finishing touches. If you encounter difficulties, refer back to your sketches and research additional tutorials online. **Step 7: Fitting** Try on your partially constructed blouse and assess the fit. Make any necessary adjustments now rather than waiting until the end. It's common to need small tweaks even with a correctly sized pattern. Be patient and persistent; achieving a perfect fit takes practice. **Step 8: Final Touches** Once satisfied with the fit, complete the remaining steps per your pattern instructions. These might include inserting sleeves, attaching collar, adding buttons or zippers, and hemming. Afterward, give your blouse a final press with an iron set at the appropriate temperature for your fabric type. Remember, designing and sewing a blouse is a learning process. Don't be discouraged by mistakes or imperfections. Each project provides valuable lessons and increases skill level. Happy sewing! Did you play with Shana at school? When Scipio saw the trouble and anxiety caused by this hasty action, he invited the people to meet him, and spoke with such pride and confidence of his youth and the duty entrusted to him and the war which he was to conduct that he awakened and renewed all the former enthusiasm, and filled his hearers with a more sanguine hope than is usually suggested by trust in promises or by inference from established facts. Scipio, indeed, did not merely deserve admiration for his genuine qualities, but from his youth upwards he had been endowed with a peculiar faculty for making the most of them. When he gave counsel to the people, he founded it on a vision of the night or an inspiration seemingly divine, either because he was in some sort influenced by superstition, or because he expected that his wishes and commands would be carried out readily if they came with a kind of oracular sanction. In very early life he began to create this impression, and as soon as he was of age, he would do no business, public or private, till he had gone to the Capitol and entered the temple, generally sitting there for a time alone and apart. By this habit, which he maintained all through his life, he gave support, either of set purpose or by accident, to a belief held by some that he was of divine parentage, and he thus revived a similar and equally baseless story, once current about Alexander the Great, that he was the son of a huge serpent, which had often been seen in the house before his birth, but glided away at the approach of any one and disappeared from sight. Title: How to Find Someone Else's Marriage License Introduction: Finding someone else's marriage license can be important for various reasons such as genealogical research, settling legal disputes, or confirming the marital status of an individual. While procedures may vary depending on the jurisdiction, this guide will provide you with general steps to locate a marriage license. Please note that privacy laws protect recent records, so access might be limited if the event occurred within the past 25-75 years, depending on your location. Step 1: Identify the Jurisdiction Determine where the couple was married. This is crucial because marriage licenses are issued by local authorities (city, county, state) rather than federally. If you know the city or town, start there. Otherwise, knowing the state should suffice. Key Tip: Some large cities have their own vital records office separate from the county they reside in. Be sure to check both if unsure. Step 2: Understand Record Availability Marriage records become public information after a certain period has passed since the date of issuance. The exact timeline varies based on the region; typically, it ranges between 25 to 75 years. For more recent records, only specific individuals involved or authorized representatives can request copies due to privacy regulations. Step 3: Gather Information About the Couple To expedite your search, gather as much relevant info about the spouses as possible. Useful details include full names (maiden name for women), approximate wedding date, place of marriage, and possibly occupations or parents' names. Step 4: Contact Local Vital Records Office Once prepared, reach out to the appropriate vital records office via phone, email, or online form. Ask whether they hold the record, what fees are associated with obtaining a copy, and any requirements necessary beforehand like identification proof or additional forms. Step 5: Meet Necessary Requirements Gather all required documents or complete any needed applications. Commonly requested items are photo ID, proof of relationship, and payment method. Fees could range from $5 upwards, payable through cash, checks, money orders, or credit cards. Step 6: Submit Your Request Submit your application either in person, by mail, or electronically following the guidance provided by the vital records office. Processing times differ but expect anywhere from immediate response to several weeks wait, especially when sending requests via post. Step 7: Receive the Response Upon receiving the response, review the returned document carefully. Confirm all details match those you were seeking. Should errors occur, contact the vital records office immediately to rectify them. Additional Guidelines: - Always double-check opening hours and holiday schedules of government buildings prior visiting. - Remember to keep personal data secure during and after your investigation. - Certain websites offer 'instant' results for purchasing marriage licenses, however, these often charge higher rates and may lack accuracy. - When dealing with older records, consider consulting historical societies, libraries, or archives. These establishments sometimes maintain indexed collections of old marriage registers or newspapers announcements which could aid your quest. We don't have any fake food at home. Adopting an animal on Club Penguin is an exciting way to support conservation efforts and add a unique touch to your penguin's igloo! By following this comprehensive guide, you will learn how to adopt an animal, understand the significance behind adoptions, and discover ways to maximize your experience. **Step 1: Log into Your Club Penguin Account** Start by visiting the official Club Penguin website and signing in using your username and password. This allows you to access your account and proceed with adopting an animal. **Step 2: Navigate to the Conservation Club Igloo** Once logged in, click on the 'IGLOOS' option at the top of the screen. From there, select the 'CATALOG' tab and choose 'FURNITURE.' Scroll down until you find the section labeled as 'Conservation Club,' then click on the igloo icon to enter the Conservation Club igloo. ![Club Penguin Catalog](https://www.clubpenguinisland.com/cpapp/web_images/catalog-header.png) **Step 3: Explore Available Animals** Inside the Conservation Club igloo, look around to see various animals that are currently available for adoption. These may include polar bears, pandas, turtles, or other endangered species. Each animal comes with its own furniture items representing their natural habitat. **Step 4: Selecting Your Desired Animal** Click on the animal you wish to adopt to view more details about them, including information regarding their real-life counterparts and the importance of conserving their habitats. Take time to read through these descriptions to make an informed decision before purchasing. **Step 5: Initiate the Adoption Process** To begin the adoption process, click on the orange button labeled 'ADOPT ME!' A popup window will appear asking if you would like to confirm your purchase. Keep in mind that adopting an animal costs coins, which serve as the virtual currency within Club Penguin. Make sure you have enough coins saved up before initiating the transaction. **Step 6: Customizing Your New Animal** After successfully adopting your chosen animal, head back to your igloo by clicking on the 'HOME' button located near the bottom left corner of the screen. Once inside, open the inventory by pressing the blue circle with a small white puffle in it (located below the map). Here, drag and drop the newly acquired animal and any accompanying furniture pieces into your igloo. Arrange them according to your preference. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** * Always ensure you have sufficient coins before attempting to adopt an animal. Prices vary depending on the type of animal, so be prepared for different cost ranges. * Be aware of potential scams when trading animals with other players. Stick to official channels such as the Club Penguin Trading Post to avoid falling victim to fraudulent activities. * Regularly check back for new animals added to the Conservation Club collection. Special events and updates often introduce limited-time adoptions, providing opportunities to collect rare creatures. * Remember that adopting an animal supports actual wildlife conservation organizations dedicated to protecting endangered species and their environments. * Interact with fellow club penguins who share similar interests in animal protection and conversation. Share knowledge and experiences while promoting awareness among peers. All those girls next door do right? Fred went into the kitchen poured his milk and spilled it. Skip to main content More Search Options A member of our team will call you back within one business day. The respiratory system carries air into and out of the lungs. When your child breathes in, oxygen-rich air flows into the lungs. This oxygen is sent to all the body's cells to use for energy and growth. When your child breathes out, waste gases flow out. Breathing in is called inspiration. When your child breathes in, air fills the airways in the lungs. Oxygen-rich air reaches the balloon-like air sacs at the end of the airways. These sacs are called alveoli. Oxygen passes into the blood vessels that surround the sacs. The blood then carries the oxygen to all parts of the body. As the body uses oxygen, it produces carbon dioxide (a waste gas), which the blood carries back to the lungs. When your child breathes out, carbon dioxide leaves the body through the airways, windpipe, and mouth or nose. Breathing out is called expiration. A child's respiratory system is similar to an adult's. However, some structures differ in size or position. For example, an infant's tongue takes up more space in the mouth than an adult's. And an infant's larynx is located in a higher position in the neck than it is in an adult. The mouth and nose are the openings through which air enters and exits the body. Sinuses are air-filled chambers within the bones of the face. They help keep the nose moist and free of dust and bacteria. The pharynx is the cavity behind the mouth. The larynx is the upper part of the windpipe, which contains the vocal cords. The windpipe (trachea) provides a pathway for air to enter and exit the lungs. The lungs are a pair of organs made of spongy tissue. They have five sections, or "lobes," three in the right lung and two in the left. The lungs allow the body to receive oxygen and get rid of carbon dioxide. Bronchioles (airways) are stretchy "branches" that transport air throughout the lungs. Bands of muscles surround each bronchiole. Bronchioles get smaller as they go deeper into the lungs. Alveoli are clusters of balloon-like air sacs at the ends of the airways. Blood vessels are tubes that carry blood to the lungs and throughout the body. Tiny blood vessels surround the air sacs, allowing an exchange of oxygen and carbon dioxide. The pleural space is an area between the lungs and chest wall, lined on both sides by tissue called pleura. The diaphragm is a muscle in the abdomen that helps with breathing. Mucus is a sticky substance made by cells in the lining of the airways. It traps dust, smoke, and other particles from air breathed in. Cilia are tiny hairs on the cells of the airway lining. They sweep mucus up the airways and to the throat. Here, mucus gets swallowed or coughed out. Can you put it in the pitcher? that's the potty makin' that noise, is n' it. Instruct him to tape or glue his pieces together to match the plan. Once he has taped his house together, provide markers or crayons for him to decorate his house. Encourage him to be creative and colorful. Place completed house on the tree wall that you completed earlier. Internal medicine doctors are in charge of inpatient units when patients are admitted for a general reason. [picture of sleeping beauty as a baby in her mother's arms] Title: How to Get Text Message Alerts from Facebook Facebook is a popular social media platform that allows users to stay connected with friends, family, and communities worldwide. One way to ensure you never miss an important update or message on Facebook is by setting up text message alerts. This tutorial will guide you through the process step-by-step so you can receive timely notifications via SMS. ### Part 1: Enabling Notification Settings on Facebook Step 1: Log into your Facebook account Visit <https://www.facebook.com/> and enter your email address or phone number along with your password to access your account. It's crucial to start with this step because without logging in, you won't be able to modify any settings related to your account. Step 2: Navigate to notification preferences Click on the arrow located at the top right corner of the screen next to the question mark icon. From the dropdown menu, select "Settings & Privacy," then click "Settings." Once you are in the Settings section, find and click "Notifications" from the left sidebar under the Preferences heading. Key Tip: Make sure you have enabled notifications for all necessary categories based on your preference. You may choose between emails, push notifications (for mobile devices), or text messages. For this tutorial, we focus on setting up text message alerts. Step 3: Choose text messaging as a method Under the "How You Get Notified" section, click "Text Messaging." Here, you can add a phone number if one isn't already listed or edit an existing one. Click "Add a Phone Number" if needed, then input the required information and confirm the addition via a code sent to your device. Guideline: Ensure that the phone number you provide is capable of receiving text messages since some landlines might not support this feature. Also, keep in mind that standard text messaging rates may apply depending on your carrier plan. ### Part 2: Customizing Text Message Alert Settings Step 4: Select alert types After adding a phone number, return to the "Text Messaging" section within Notifications Settings. Under "What Notifications You Get," customize which updates you want to receive via SMS. The options include comments, friend requests, birthdays, event reminders, and more. Step 5: Adjust frequency controls Still within the same section, adjust the frequency control option according to your preference. You can opt to receive individual notifications immediately or bundled ones every few hours. Keep in mind that frequent alerts might consume data quickly if you don't have unlimited data plans. Key Tip: Consider enabling additional security features such as login alerts to protect your account further. These alerts notify you whenever someone logs into your account from an unknown browser or device. ### Part 3: Verifying Your Setup Step 6: Save changes Double-check all modifications made to your notification settings before proceeding. Once satisfied, scroll down and click "Save Changes" to finalize your setup. Step 7: Test your new settings To verify that everything works correctly, perform a test action relevant to the alerts you chose earlier. For example, ask a friend to comment on one of your posts and check whether you receive a corresponding SMS notification. In conclusion, setting up text message alerts from Facebook ensures that you remain updated even when away from your computer or smartphone. By following these steps meticulously, you'll enjoy seamless connectivity with your online community while managing potential distractions effectively. Title: How to Perform a Round-Off in Gymnastics A round-off is a fundamental skill in gymnastics that serves as a building block for more advanced tumbling passes. It involves two consecutive 180-degree turns (one in the air and one on the ground) while landing with both feet at the same time. This tutorial will guide you through performing a round-off correctly and safely. **Things You'll Need:** * A safe environment with enough space to perform a round-off, such as a padded floor or mat * Comfortable athletic clothing * Properly fitted gymnastics shoes (optional) **Step 1: Stance and Preparation** Start by standing straight with your feet shoulder-width apart. Bend your knees slightly and place your hands flat on the floor about shoulder-width apart. Your body should form an L shape when viewed from the side. Keeping your legs together, lift your heels off the ground so that only the balls of your feet are touching. This position prepares your body for the explosive push needed to execute a successful round-off. By keeping your legs together, you ensure proper alignment throughout the move, reducing the risk of injury. **Key Tip**: Engage your core muscles to maintain balance and stability during this stance. **Step 2: The Push-Off** From the initial lunge position, rapidly extend your hips, propelling yourself forward into the air. As you jump, snap your wrists downward to generate extra power. Simultaneously, kick your legs up behind you, striving to reach a handstand position mid-air. Remember to keep your legs together throughout this motion. The force generated from the push-off allows you to rotate quickly enough to complete the first 180 degrees turn before making contact with the ground again. **Key Guideline**: Practice pushing off harder than necessary until you can consistently land securely on your feet after completing the full rotation. **Step 3: The Landing** As you descend from the peak of your jump, bend your arms to absorb some of the impact. At the same time, begin bending your knees and hips to prepare for the final part of the round-off - sticking the landing. Your goal here is to make sure both feet hit the ground simultaneously. To do this, focus on extending your legs fully upon landing. Also, try to distribute your weight evenly between both feet. **Key Tip**: Imagine there's a line drawn horizontally across the middle of your body. When you land, visualize splitting that line directly over the centerline of your body. **Step 4: Straightening Up** After successfully sticking the landing, rise back up to stand tall. Make sure to roll your shoulders back and down to prevent hunching over. Check your posture; if done correctly, you should be able to stand upright without any discomfort. Maintaining good posture ensures that you don't put unnecessary strain on your spine and helps reinforce correct muscle memory for future attempts. **Precautions** Always warm up properly before attempting any physical activity like gymnastics. Start slowly and gradually increase intensity to avoid injuries. If possible, learn under the supervision of a trained coach who can provide feedback and assistance. Lastly, remember that practice makes perfect. Don't get discouraged if you struggle initially; persistence pays off! No you don't wanna play with my dirty shoes. [mother whispers boy boy boy.] Title: How to Choose Exercise Machines for Chronic Hip Pain Introduction: Exercising with chronic hip pain can be challenging, but it's not impossible. The right exercise machine can help you strengthen your muscles, improve flexibility, and reduce pain. This tutorial will guide you through choosing appropriate exercise machines based on your needs and abilities, enabling you to manage your chronic hip pain effectively. Step 1: Consult Your Healthcare Provider Before starting any new exercise routine, consult your healthcare provider or physical therapist. They can assess your condition, recommend suitable exercises, and provide guidance on using exercise machines safely. Key Tip: Be honest about your pain levels and limitations, so your healthcare provider can give accurate advice tailored to your situation. Step 2: Understand Your Hip Pain Determine the cause and location of your hip pain (e.g., joint, muscle, tendon). This information will influence which machines are safe and beneficial for you. Common causes include arthritis, bursitis, tendinitis, and hip labral tears. Guideline: If you have difficulty identifying the source of your pain, seek professional assistance from a medical expert. Step 3: Prioritize Low-Impact Machines Low-impact exercise machines minimize stress on your hips by reducing the amount of weight and force applied during workouts. Examples include recumbent bicycles, elliptical trainers, and rowing machines. Key Benefit: These machines allow you to maintain an active lifestyle while protecting your hips from further damage. Step 4: Consider Seated Machines Seated machines like recumbent bicycles, leg presses, and seated rowers enable you to exercise without putting excessive pressure on your hips. Additionally, they offer better back support than standing machines. Benefits: Improved posture, reduced strain on the lower back, and controlled movement that minimizes the risk of injury. Step 5: Adjust Machine Settings Accordingly Ensure that the seat height, resistance level, and other settings are adjusted correctly before beginning your workout. Proper positioning reduces unnecessary strain on your hips and enhances overall effectiveness. Key Tips: Start with low resistance and gradually increase as needed. Maintain proper form throughout your exercise session. Step 6: Gradually Build Endurance and Strength Begin with short workout sessions at a comfortable intensity. Over time, slowly build up endurance and strength by increasing duration, frequency, and resistance. Consistency is crucial for long-term pain management. Guidelines: Aim for 10-15 minutes per session initially, then add five minutes every week until you reach 30 minutes. Perform exercises three to four times weekly. Step 7: Monitor Progress and Listen to Your Body Keep track of your progress and adjust your exercise plan accordingly. Pay attention to changes in pain levels, mobility, and overall wellbeing. Stop exercising immediately if you experience increased pain or discomfort. Key Tip: Remember that some soreness after working out is normal; however, sharp or persistent pain should prompt you to rest and reassess your approach. Conclusion: By carefully selecting and utilizing exercise machines suited to your specific needs, you can effectively manage chronic hip pain while maintaining an active lifestyle. Always prioritize safety, listen to your body, and seek professional advice when necessary. With patience and persistence, you can overcome hip pain and enjoy improved quality of life. A time in the 24-hour clock is written in the form hours:minutes (for example, 01:23), or hours:minutes:seconds (01:23:45). Numbers under 10 usually have a zero in front (called a leading zero); e.g. 09:07. Under the 24-hour clock system, the day begins at midnight, 00:00, and the last minute of the day begins at 23:59 and ends at 24:00, which is identical to 00:00 of the following day. 12:00 can only be mid-day. Midnight is called 24:00 and is used to mean the end of the day and 00:00 is used to mean the beginning of the day. For example, you would say "Tuesday at 24:00" and "Wednesday at 00:00" to mean exactly the same time. Build a blimp and become an aerospace engineer! In this hands-on activity, kids explore the physics of force by creating balloon blimps with a propulsion system. They problem solve how to make their blimp buoyant while being propelled forward. The Girlstart girls enjoyed testing different amounts of weight added to their blimps to make them buoyant and propel specific distances. Students compare and explain forces that act on air-filled and helium balloons. Students use both types of balloons to create a blimp that is neutrally buoyant and then modify their design with a propulsion system that will propel their blimp forward. |(4.5A)||Measure, compare, and contrast physical properties of matter, including size, mass, volume, states (solid, liquid, gas), temperature, magnetism, and the ability to sink or float| |(5.6D)||Design an experiment that tests the effect of force on an object.| |(4.6D)||Design an experiment to test the effect of force on an object such as a push or a pull, gravity, friction, or magnetism.| - Aeronautic Engineer poster - Pre-measured weights of paper clips For each group (2-3 students) - 2 9in balloons filled with helium - Clear tape - Duct tape - Straws (not flexible) - Large paper clips - Small paper clips - White copy paper - Meter stick or tape. For each student |buoyancy||the ability of matter to float in a liquid or gas| |force||a push or pull| |gravity||A force that pulls all objects toward each other| |hover||to remain floating or suspended in the air| |mass||the amount of matter in an object| |motion||a change of position| |neutral buoyancy||condition in which an object's mass equals the mass it displaces in its surround medium; the object will neither sink nor rise| |propel||to cause to move forward; to launch with great force| |propulsion||the process of causing something to move by applying a force against it.| - Conduct the activities prior to teaching the lesson. The blimp is constructed using two 9in balloons because one balloon will not float once the propulsion system is attached. - Using Helium tank, per-fill 2 balloons/group before club. - Students may inflate balloons by blowing into the straws, but they will need to switch out straws so that each student has their own to blow into. - Make copies of Build a Blimp Results page. - Make copies of Parts of a Blimp diagram. Aerospace engineers create machines, from airplanes to spacecraft. They design, develop, and test aircraft, spacecraft, and missiles and supervise the manufacture of these products. Aerospace engineers who work with aircraft are called aeronautical engineers. - Hold up two balloons - one filled with air and one filled with helium. Let both balloons go. Ask students to explain the difference between the balloons. Answers might include - the balloon that floats is filled with helium gas. The balloon that sinks is filled with air. - Ask students if they think it is possible to design a system to make the balloons travel across the room instead of up or down. Ask and discuss: - What special type of balloon can move horizontally? A blimp moves forward over the ground. The Goodyear blimp is a great example. They can be seen flying above stadiums during football games. Students will look at a diagram of a blimp later in the lesson. - What are blimps used for? Blimps used to be used for military purposes. Today blimps are used for advertising, TV coverage, and tourism. - Before students begin brainstorming ways to make the balloons change direction they will need to understand how the two different balloons work. Ask and discuss: - What force causes the air-filled balloon to sink? Gravity pulls on both balloons, but since the balloon filled with air is heavier than air by itself, it sinks to the ground. - Why does the balloon filled with helium float? The helium gas is lighter than air. - What other forces can cause a balloon to change its motion? Blowing wind and hitting a balloon can cause it to change motion. - What would happen to the helium balloon if we went outside and released it? Students may share personal experiences of watching balloons float upward until gone from view. See background information for an example of a helium balloon that reaches the top of the atmosphere. - How is the air like water? If you put an object such as a baseball in a pool, the ball will sink. If you put an empty bottle in a pool, the bottle will float at the top because the air filled bottle is lighter than the water. Compare the helium balloon to the bottle in the pool. The balloon will float toward the top of the atmosphere like the bottle will float to the top of the water. - Introduce the challenge. Students will create a blimp that will travel forward across the room while displaying an advertisement. Students will need to control the forces acting on the blimp so that it will move forward. - Organize students into groups of two or three. Give each group two Mylar balloons, straws and clear tape. Ask and discuss: - How can you connect the balloons so they move as one system? The lesson shows how to connect the balloons using one straw, but students may have other ideas they would like to try. Students must be careful not to add too much mass to the system. Otherwise the blimp will start to sink. - How can you get the balloons to hover? Using the directions below, students will find that the blimp will still rise upward. They many suggest adding mass to help the balloon float in the air. - Groups follow their plan or the plan outlined below to connect their balloons and make the blimp hover. |Students turn the Mylar balloons upside down and tape them to a straw so that one balloon sits behind the other. Students may use other methods of connecting balloons such as rolled up paper or skewers.||Turn the Mylar balloons upside down and tape them to a straw so that one balloon sits behind the other. Make sure the straw is taped securely to both balloons. This creates a frame for the blimp.| |When students hold the straw, the balloons should sit next to each other.||One way to arrange the balloons is to position them behind one another. You can also place them side by side, but you will need to modify your straw so that it is long enough to connect the balloons.| |Students will propel the blimp using a propulsion system made with a straw and latex balloon. Slip one end of the straw into the balloon. Tape the balloon to the straw using duct tape.||Your balloon needs a something to propel it forward. Ask and discuss: You will use a straw and latex balloon for your propulsion system. Slip one end of the straw into the balloon. Tape the balloon to the straw using duct tape. |Tape the propulsion system to the bottom of the straw frame that connects the two Mylar balloons. |Tape the propulsion system to the bottom of the straw frame that connects the two Mylar balloons. Be sure to leave enough space at the open end of the straw so you can pump or blow air into the balloon. |If students let go of the blimp, it will probably rise into the air. Provide students with large and small paper clips. Students will add paper clips until their blimp hovers. Students will know they are successful when the blimp hovers for at least five seconds before slowly sinking close to the floor or rising close to the ceiling. Even if the blimp eventually moves close to the ceiling or floor, it can still be considered as hovering.||If students let go, the balloons will probably rise into the air. Ask: Add paper clips until your blimp hovers. You will know you are successful when the blimp hovers for at least five seconds. |Depending on drafts in the room, the blimp may float higher toward the ceiling or lower toward the ground. If it continues to float and stay at the ceiling, then students should add more paper clips. If it sinks to the ground and stays there, students should remove paper clips.||Depending on drafts in the room, the blimp may float higher toward the ceiling or lower toward the ground. If it floats to the ceiling, then you should add more paper clips. If it sinks to the ground and stays there, you should remove paper clips.| |Students measure the mass of the paperclips.||Measure the mass of the paper clips when you have found the combination that allows your blimp to hover.| - Groups demonstrate their hovering blimps. Create a table on the board to record the mass required for each blimp to hover in the air. - Why is the balloon able to float? Adding mass to the balloon makes it heavier. This allows gravity to pull the balloon toward the ground. Explain that the balloon is still buoyant, but it becomes neutrally buoyant when it neither sinks nor rises upward. - What forces are being applied to the balloon? Gravity is pulling down on the balloon. The items attached to the balloon help to weigh it down. - What propels a blimp? Give each students a Parts of a Blimp diagram. Read the article and discuss how the parts work together to provide thrust and lift for the blimp. - What force could we apply to the balloon to make it move across the room? Students will explain that by filling the latex balloon with air and then letting go of the straw, the force of the air leaving the propulsion system will push the blimp forward. On the board, draw a diagram showing the force of air moving in one direction, and the motion of the blimp in the opposite direction. - Remind students that their challenge is to control the forces acting on the blimp so that it can travel across the floor while displaying an advertisement. - Ask students to discuss in their groups the modifications they will need to make so their blimp will travel across the room to their partner. Students may refer the Parts of Blimp diagram for ideas. Students' ideas may include: - Adding tail fins: rudders and elevators - Adding wings - Changing the position of the balloons - Making a new connection between the balloons - Adding more balloon straws - Create a class list of modifications students may make based on each group's discussion. - Students may cut fins, rudders, or other pieces out of white copy paper to help direct the blimp as it moves forward. Students need to keep in mind that any changes they make will affect the overall weight of the blimp. They must be sure not to weigh it down to the ground or let it float up to the ceiling. - Each group's final touch will be to add an advertisement to their blimp. - Students test their blimp and make adjustments based on their results. - Each group describes their design and demonstrates their blimp. Ask and discuss: - How well did your propulsion system work? Did you have to make any changes? Answers will be based on results. - How did you modify your blimp so that it could fly straight? Answers will be based on results. - What would you still like to change in your design? Why? Answers will be based on results. - Students complete reflections in their Engineer's Journal. - If time permits, ask students to consider what would happen if they connected all of the balloons. How much mass would they need to add to the super blimp to make it hover above the ground? How helium balloons work Design Squad: Blimp Jet Helium balloons work because of buoyancy. Buoyancy is the ability of matter to float in a liquid or gas. Helium is a lot lighter than air, so a helium balloon that you hold by a string is floating in a "pool" of air and displacing an amount of air. As long as the helium plus the balloon is lighter than the air it displaces, the balloon will float. Helium weighs 0.1785 grams per liter. Nitrogen weighs 1.2506 grams per liter, and since nitrogen makes up about 80 percent of the air we breathe, 1.25 grams is a good approximation for the weight of a liter of air. A balloon that is one foot (30 centimeters) in diameter holds about half a cubic foot of helium, which is the same as 14 liters. This means the balloon will weigh about half an ounce (14 grams) less than the same sized balloon filled with air. Because of its buoyancy the helium balloon floats upward. Blimps and balloons are generally quite large because they have to displace a lot of air to float. If students are curious about how high a helium balloon will float in the air, here is an interesting experiment that films a helium balloon traveling to the top of the atmosphere before popping. http://blogs.howstuffworks.com/2010/10/22/when-i-let-a-balloon-go-how-high-can-it-go/ Girlstart is an award-winning Austin-based non-profit organization dedicated to empowering girls in science, technology, engineering, and math (STEM). This is because it reacts with air to form sodium hydroxide and sodium carbonate. Sodium is a little lighter than water; when it reacts with water it floats. This reaction is very fast. Hydrogen and sodium hydroxide are produced. The hydrogen may ignite. Since sodium melts at a low temperature, it melts when it reacts with water. It has one valence electron which is removed easily, making it highly reactive. Title: How to Conduct a Conference Call on an Android Device Introduction: In today's fast-paced world, conference calls have become an essential tool for businesses, remote teams, and individuals who need to communicate with multiple people at once. This tutorial will guide you through the process of conducting a conference call on your Android device, providing in-depth explanations for each step along with key tips and guidelines for ensuring clarity and practicality. Step 1: Choosing a Conference Call Service Before initiating a conference call, select a reliable service that suits your needs. There are numerous free and paid options available, such as Google Meet, Skype, Zoom, and Microsoft Teams. These services offer various features like screen sharing, video conferencing, and recording capabilities. Research and choose one based on factors like ease of use, number of participants allowed, and security measures provided. Key Tips: * Test different platforms beforehand to determine which works best for you and your group. * Consider compatibility issues between devices when selecting a platform (e.g., some may work better for iOS users than others). Step 2: Setting Up Your Account Once you've chosen a service, sign up or log into your account. For most platforms, this requires entering basic personal information and creating a username and password. After signing up, familiarize yourself with the user interface and settings so you can efficiently manage your conference calls. Key Guidelines: * Keep your login credentials secure and confidential. * Make sure to read the terms of service and privacy policies of the selected platform. Step 3: Inviting Participants To invite participants, open the app and navigate to its scheduling or invitation feature. Enter the email addresses of those you want to include, along with any additional details about the meeting time, date, agenda, and dial-in numbers if necessary. Send out invitations well in advance so attendees can prepare accordingly. Key Tips: * Use BCC when sending mass emails to protect recipients' privacy. * Confirm receipt of invites and provide alternative contact methods for those experiencing technical difficulties. Step 4: Preparing Your Devices Ensure your Android device is fully charged or connected to power during the conference call. Additionally, connect to a stable Wi-Fi network to avoid data usage charges and maintain consistent audio quality. If possible, use headphones with a built-in microphone to reduce background noise and improve sound reception. Key Guidelines: * Close unnecessary apps running in the background to conserve battery life and memory resources. * Disable notification sounds and vibration alerts to minimize distractions during the call. Step 5: Initiating the Conference Call Launch the chosen app on your Android device and join the scheduled meeting by clicking on the appropriate link or entering the corresponding meeting ID. Once connected, mute your mic until ready to speak to prevent unwanted interruptions from ambient noise. Key Tips: * Familiarize yourself with the app's controls and functions prior to starting the call. * Encourage other participants to do the same to ensure smooth communication throughout the session. Title: How to Diagnose Huntington's Disease Huntington's disease is an inherited neurodegenerative disorder that affects muscle coordination and leads to cognitive decline and psychiatric problems. This article will guide you through the process of diagnosing this condition. Please note that only qualified healthcare professionals can make a definitive diagnosis; however, understanding the process may help you support a loved one or recognize symptoms early. **Step 1: Recognize Symptoms** The first step in diagnosing Huntington's disease is recognizing its symptoms. These typically include: - Uncontrolled movements (chorea) - Mood changes, such as depression, irritability, anxiety, or psychosis - Cognitive difficulties, including memory loss and difficulty swallowing, speaking, or organizing thoughts These symptoms usually appear between ages 30 and 50 but can start earlier or later. If these signs are present, consider seeking medical advice. **Step 2: Consult a Medical Professional** If you suspect someone has Huntington's disease based on observed symptoms, encourage them to consult with their primary care physician or a neurologist. They will conduct initial assessments and possibly refer the individual to specialists if necessary. Key Tips: - Be honest about all symptoms, even those that seem unrelated. - Bring up any family history of neurological disorders. **Step 3: Initial Assessments** During the initial consultation, the doctor will perform physical examinations and gather information about personal and familial medical histories. They might also request basic laboratory tests to rule out other conditions causing similar symptoms. **Step 4: Neurological Examinations** Neurologists play a crucial role in diagnosing Huntington's disease due to their expertise in nervous system disorders. After the preliminary assessment, they may perform more specialized evaluations like: - Observation of involuntary movements - Checking reflexes, balance, and coordination - Testing cognitive abilities, such as memory, problem solving, and concentration **Step 5: Genetic Testing** Genetic testing is currently the most reliable method for confirming Huntington's disease. A blood sample is taken and analyzed for the presence of the expanded HTT gene mutation responsible for the disorder. It's important to remember that a negative result does not guarantee absence of the disease because symptoms could still develop late in life. Guidelines: - Consider genetic counseling before undergoing testing to fully understand implications. - Remember, results affect not just the person being tested but potentially also their relatives. **Step 6: Imaging Studies** Imaging techniques like Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI), Computerized Tomography (CT), or Positron Emission Tomography (PET) scans can provide visual evidence of brain abnormalities associated with Huntington's disease. However, these cannot conclusively diagnose the condition alone. **Step 7: Psychiatric Evaluation** Since mental health issues often accompany Huntington's disease, consulting a psychiatrist or psychologist can be beneficial. Proper evaluation and management of psychological aspects can significantly improve patients' quality of life. Remember, while this article provides insight into diagnosing Huntington's disease, it should never replace professional medical opinion. Early detection can lead to better symptom management and planning for future care needs. Now you may hang here until you get a little sense in your thick head. You may hang here and watch while I go and talk with Teeka." Taug blustered and threatened, but Tarzan only grinned at him as he dropped lightly to the lower levels. He sought to placate her; he urged his friendly intentions, and craned his neck to have a look at Teeka's balu; but the she-ape was not to be persuaded that he meant other than harm to her little one. A person who understands the scientific method can test to see if the facts of a scientific theory are correct. When statements have been tested and shown to be not false, they are said to be "true". The facts of each scientific theory have usually been questioned and tested for a very long time by many people. However, the critical point is that an agreed method has been used to show that the statement is not false. Because of this, we know that the knowledge of a scientific theory is fact and not opinion. Examples of scientific theories include the theory of relativity and the theory of evolution. No you just sit on that one. Oh my goodness that was a scary dream.
